12	      "I have all the verified facts I need — this is a greenfield dir (no `go.mod` yet), and the research locks the stack. Here is the concrete, implementable design.\n\n---\n\n# translate — TUI + Interaction Layer Design\n\n## 0. Stack pin (put in `go.mod` / CLAUDE.md loudly)\n\nGo 1.25. Charm **v2 on `charm.land/*`** (NOT `github.com/charmbracelet/*` — v1 codegen will not compile):\n\n```\ncharm.land/bubbletea/v2 v2.0.8\ncharm.land/bubbles/v2   v2.0.x   // textarea, textinput, viewport, list, spinner, help, key\ncharm.land/lipgloss/v2  v2.0.5\ncharm.land/huh/v2       v2.0.3   // init wizard only\ngithub.com/spf13/cobra           // unaffected by v2\ngithub.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2  // config\ngithub.com/sahilm/fuzzy          // history + language-name fuzz\ngithub.com/abadojack/whatlanggo  // offline detect fallback\ngolang.org/x/term                // IsTerminal\ngithub.com/atotto/clipboard      // copy result (osc52 fallback optional)\n```\n\nv2 reminders baked into the sketches below: `Update` returns `(tea.Model, tea.Cmd)` unchanged; `View() tea.View` (wrap with `tea.NewView(string)`); keys arrive as `tea.KeyPressMsg` (`msg.String()` still matches).\n\n## 1. Package layout\n\n```\ntranslate/\n├── main.go                       // cobra root + Execute()\n├── cmd/\n│   ├── root.go                   // run(): args/stdin/tty dispatch (§8)\n│   └── init.go                   // `translate init` -> huh standalone wizard + probes\n├── internal/\n│   ├── config/                   // TOML load/save, XDG resolver (§ below), defaults\n│   │   ├── xdg.go                // configHome()/dataHome()/stateHome() ~/.config resolver\n│   │   ├── config.go             // Config, Provider structs; Load/Save/EnsureDirs\n│   │   └── state.go              // state.json: LastSource/LastTarget/DebounceOn\n│   ├── engine/                   // ENGINE LAYER — shared by CLI + TUI, zero tea imports\n│   │   ├── engine.go             // Engine interface, TranslateResult, Request\n│   │   ├── chain.go              // fallback chain (copilot -> ollama -> google)\n│   │   ├── llm.go                // OpenAI-compat client (copilot/ollama/openrouter/litellm)\n│   │   ├── google.go            // googleapis translate_a/single free path\n│   │   ├── mymemory.go           // secondary free path\n│   │   ├── dict.go               // dictionaryapi.dev exact -> local Levenshtein fuzzy\n│   │   ├── detect.go             // whatlanggo Detector\n│   │   └── prompt.go             // built-in system prompts + JSON schema/response_format\n│   ├── lang/                     // ISO code table + fuzzy resolver (\"chinees\"->zh)\n│   ├── store/                    // Store interface; JSONL impl (sqlite drop-in later)\n│   └── tui/\n│       ├── model.go              // Model struct, New(), Init()\n│       ├── update.go             // Update() switch\n│       ├── view.go               // View() + layout\n│       ├── msgs.go               // all custom msg structs + Cmds\n│       ├── keys.go               // keyMap (bubbles/v2/key) + help bindings\n│       └── styles.go             // lipgloss styles, recompute on resize\n└── go.mod\n```\n\n**Key seam:** `engine.Engine` and `store.Store` are pure Go with `context.Context` — the TUI and the one-shot CLI both call the identical `engine.Chain.Translate(ctx, Request)`. Bubble Tea exists only under `internal/tui`.\n\n### Engine contract (shared spine — both modes)\n\n```go\n// internal/engine/engine.go\ntype TranslateResult struct {              // the \"Marvin-lite\" typed struct\n    Translation    string   `json:\"translation\"`\n    DetectedSource string   `json:\"detected_source\"`\n    Target         string   `json:\"target\"`\n    Alternatives   []string `json:\"alternatives,omitempty\"`\n    Notes          string   `json:\"notes,omitempty\"`\n    Confidence     float64  `json:\"confidence,omitempty\"`\n    Engine         string   `json:\"engine\"`     // which backend produced it\n    Model          string   `json:\"model,omitempty\"`\n}\n\ntype Request struct {\n    Text        string\n    Source      string   // \"\" or \"auto\"\n    Target      string\n    Interpret   bool     // typo-tolerant \"interpret intended meaning\" prompt\n}\n\n// Stream is optional; nil onToken => non-streaming engines return whole result.\ntype Engine interface {\n    Name() string\n    Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request, onToken func(string)) (TranslateResult, error)\n    Probe(ctx context.Context) error   // for `init` wizard + chain health\n}\n```\n\n- `onToken != nil` and engine can stream (LLM SSE) → engine calls `onToken(delta)` per chunk AND returns the assembled final result. Google/MyMemory/dict ignore `onToken` and return once.\n- `Chain.Translate` iterates engines, calling `Probe`-cheap or just attempting; on error falls to next; the winning engine stamps `result.Engine`.\n\n## 2. Bubble Tea Model struct\n\n```go\n// internal/tui/model.go\ntype Model struct {\n    // ---- components ----\n    ta      textarea.Model     // source input (left-top)\n    vp      viewport.Model     // result render (left-bottom), scrollable\n    hist    list.Model         // history picker (right pane / overlay)\n    sp      spinner.Model      // shown while in-flight\n    help    help.Model\n    keys    keyMap\n\n    // ---- engine wiring (shared layer) ----\n    chain   *engine.Chain\n    store   store.Store\n\n    // ---- request lifecycle / debounce (single source of truth) ----\n    seq      int                  // monotonic generation counter\n    inflight int                  // seq of the request currently running (0 = none)\n    cancel   context.CancelFunc   // cancels the in-flight engine call\n    stream   chan streamEvent     // engine goroutine -> UI\n\n    // ---- streaming / result state ----\n    buf      strings.Builder      // live streaming token buffer\n    result   engine.TranslateResult\n    lastErr  error\n\n    // ---- language + engine selection ----\n    source     string             // \"auto\" or code\n    target     string\n    engineName string             // \"\" = chain/auto, or forced engine id\n    engineIdx  int                // index into cyclable engine list\n    model      string             // current LLM model id (for LLM engines)\n\n    // ---- flags / modes ----\n    debounceOn bool\n    focus      focusArea          // focusInput | focusHistory\n    showHistory bool\n    status     statusKind         // idle | typing | translating | done | errored\n\n    // ---- layout ----\n    width, height int\n    ready         bool            // first WindowSizeMsg seen\n}\n\ntype focusArea int\nconst ( focusInput focusArea = iota; focusHistory )\n\ntype statusKind int\nconst ( statIdle statusKind = iota; statTyping; statTranslating; statDone; statErr )\n```\n\n`Init()`:\n```go\nfunc (m Model) Init() tea.Cmd {\n    return tea.Batch(\n        textarea.Blink,        // v2 cursor blink cmd\n        m.sp.Tick,\n        tea.RequestWindowSize, // v2 name\n        tea.RequestBackgroundColor, // for light/dark adaptivity\n        loadHistoryCmd(m.store),\n    )\n}\n```\n\n## 3. Full message set (`msgs.go`)\n\n```go\n// ---- debounce / request lifecycle ----\ntype debounceMsg struct{ seq int }                     // fired 400ms after keystroke\ntype streamEvent struct{ seq int; text string }        // one streamed token\ntype doneMsg  struct{ seq int; res engine.TranslateResult } // final result\ntype errMsg   struct{ seq int; err error }             // engine failed (chain exhausted)\n\n// ---- data loads ----\ntype historyLoadedMsg struct{ items []store.Entry }\ntype historySavedMsg  struct{ err error }\n\n// ---- side effects ----\ntype copiedMsg  struct{ err error }\ntype clearErrMsg struct{}                              // timed clear of transient error\n\n// ---- environment ----\n// tea.WindowSizeMsg          (builtin, unchanged in v2)\n// tea.KeyPressMsg            (builtin v2; was KeyMsg)\n// tea.BackgroundColorMsg     (builtin v2; msg.IsDark())\ntype bgMsg = tea.BackgroundColorMsg\n```\n\n### Cmds\n\n```go\n// debounce timer — one Tick per keystroke, collapsed by seq guard\nfunc armDebounce(seq int) tea.Cmd {\n    return tea.Tick(400*time.Millisecond, func(time.Time) tea.Msg {\n        return debounceMsg{seq: seq}\n    })\n}\n\n// self-resubscribing stream reader (Charm realtime pattern)\nfunc waitStream(ch chan streamEvent, seq int) tea.Cmd {\n    return func() tea.Msg {\n        ev, ok := <-ch\n        if !ok { return nil } // closed handled via sentinel below\n        return ev\n    }\n}\n```\n\n`launch` (the one place that starts a translation) — bumps seq, cancels prior, spins goroutine:\n\n```go\nfunc (m *Model) launch() tea.Cmd {\n    text := strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value())\n    if text == \"\" { m.status = statIdle; return nil }\n\n    m.seq++\n    if m.cancel != nil { m.cancel() }          // cancel prior in-flight\n    ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n    ch := make(chan streamEvent, 32)\n    m.cancel, m.stream = cancel, ch\n    m.inflight = m.seq\n    m.buf.Reset(); m.lastErr = nil; m.status = statTranslating\n\n    seq := m.seq\n    req := engine.Request{Text: text, Source: m.source, Target: m.target, Interpret: true}\n    eng := m.selectedEngine()  // forced engine or the chain\n\n    go func() {\n        defer close(ch)\n        res, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req, func(tok string) {\n            select {\n            case ch <- streamEvent{seq: seq, text: tok}:\n            case <-ctx.Done():\n            }\n        })\n        // final delivery multiplexed onto the same channel via a wrapper below,\n        // OR sent as a separate tea.Msg from a second Cmd — we use the latter:\n        finalCh <- ... // see note\n    }()\n    return tea.Batch(m.sp.Tick, waitStream(ch, seq), collectFinal(ctx, ...))\n}\n```\n\n**Delivery detail (chosen approach):** run the engine in the goroutine and push tokens onto the buffered `stream` channel; when the goroutine finishes it sends the terminal `doneMsg`/`errMsg` by closing the channel with the last event carrying the assembled result. Concretely, `streamEvent` grows a `final *engine.TranslateResult` and `err error` sentinel; `waitStream` translates the closed/sentinel case into `doneMsg`/`errMsg`:\n\n```go\ntype streamEvent struct {\n    seq   int\n    text  string                    // \"\" on terminal event\n    final *engine.TranslateResult   // non-nil => done\n    err   error                     // non-nil => error\n}\n\nfunc waitStream(ch chan streamEvent, seq int) tea.Cmd {\n    return func() tea.Msg {\n        ev, ok := <-ch\n        if !ok { return nil }\n        switch {\n        case ev.err != nil:     return errMsg{seq, ev.err}\n        case ev.final != nil:   return doneMsg{seq, *ev.final}\n        default:                return ev            // a token\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\nThis keeps **one channel, one seq, one reader** — no second goroutine to coordinate.\n\n## 4. Update flow (`update.go`)\n\n```go\nfunc (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {\n    switch msg := msg.(type) {\n\n    case tea.WindowSizeMsg:\n        m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height\n        m.relayout()                       // §7 recompute all component sizes\n        m.ready = true\n        return m, nil\n\n    case bgMsg:\n        applyTheme(msg.IsDark())           // rebuild styles\n        return m, nil\n\n    case tea.KeyPressMsg:\n        return m.handleKey(msg)\n\n    case debounceMsg:\n        if msg.seq != m.seq { return m, nil }   // (a) newer keystroke arrived -> ignore\n        return m, m.launch()\n\n    case streamEvent:                            // a live token\n        if msg.seq != m.inflight { return m, nil } // (c) stale/cancelled -> drop\n        m.buf.WriteString(msg.text)\n        m.vp.SetContent(renderStreaming(m.buf.String()))\n        m.vp.GotoBottom()\n        return m, waitStream(m.stream, m.inflight) // re-subscribe for next token\n\n    case doneMsg:\n        if msg.seq != m.inflight { return m, nil }\n        m.result, m.status, m.inflight = msg.res, statDone, 0\n        m.vp.SetContent(renderResult(m.result))   // full structured render\n        return m, saveHistoryCmd(m.store, m.result)\n\n    case errMsg:\n        if msg.seq != m.inflight { return m, nil }\n        m.lastErr, m.status, m.inflight = msg.err, statErr, 0\n        return m, tea.Tick(4*time.Second, func(time.Time) tea.Msg { return clearErrMsg{} })\n\n    case clearErrMsg:\n        if m.status == statErr { m.status = statIdle; m.lastErr = nil }\n        return m, nil\n\n    case historyLoadedMsg:\n        m.hist.SetItems(toListItems(msg.items))\n        return m, nil\n\n    case copiedMsg:\n        m.flashStatus(\"copied ✓\"); return m, nil\n    }\n\n    // fall-through: let focused component consume it (spinner tick, list nav, etc.)\n    return m.updateFocused(msg)\n}\n```\n\n### `handleKey` — keystroke vs hotkey split\n\n```go\nfunc (m Model) handleKey(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {\n    // Global hotkeys FIRST (work regardless of focus)\n    switch {\n    case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Quit):        return m, tea.Quit\n    case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.ToggleLive):  m.debounceOn = !m.debounceOn; return m, nil\n    case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.CycleEngine): m.cycleEngine();  return m, m.launch() // re-run with new engine\n    case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.SwapLang):    m.source, m.target = resolveSwap(m); return m, m.launch()\n    case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.PickTarget):  return m, m.openTargetPicker()          // huh-select or inline list\n    case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.History):     m.showHistory = !m.showHistory; m.focus = focusHistory; return m, nil\n    case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Copy):        return m, copyCmd(m.result.Translation)\n    }\n\n    // History pane focused: Enter = load selected into input\n    if m.focus == focusHistory {\n        switch {\n        case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Confirm):\n            e := m.hist.SelectedItem().(histItem).entry\n            m.ta.SetValue(e.Source)\n            m.source, m.target = e.DetectedSource, e.Target\n            m.focus, m.showHistory = focusInput, false\n            return m, m.launch()                 // immediate re-translate of recalled text\n        case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Cancel):\n            m.focus, m.showHistory = focusInput, false; return m, nil\n        }\n        var cmd tea.Cmd\n        m.hist, cmd = m.hist.Update(msg)         // arrow nav / filter\n        return m, cmd\n    }\n\n    // Input focused\n    if key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Translate) {      // Enter -> immediate\n        return m, m.launch()\n    }\n\n    // Ordinary keystroke -> update textarea, (re)arm debounce\n    var cmd tea.Cmd\n    m.ta, cmd = m.ta.Update(msg)\n    m.status = statTyping\n    if m.debounceOn && m.ta.Value() != \"\" {\n        m.seq++                                  // (a) invalidate any pending debounceMsg\n        if m.cancel != nil { m.cancel() }        // (b) cancel in-flight engine work\n        m.inflight = 0\n        return m, tea.Batch(cmd, armDebounce(m.seq))\n    }\n    return m, cmd\n}\n```\n\n**Note on Enter vs newline:** textarea normally inserts `\\n` on Enter. Bind `Translate` to **Enter** and multiline-insert to **Alt+Enter** (or Ctrl+J), so `Enter=translate immediately` matches the locked spec. Configurable in `keyMap`.\n\n## 5. Stale / cancelled result discard (the core invariant)\n\n**One monotonic `m.seq` is the single source of truth.** Three effects hang off it:\n\n1. **Debounce collapse:** every keystroke does `m.seq++` then `armDebounce(m.seq)`. When a `debounceMsg{seq}` arrives, `if msg.seq != m.seq { return }` — only the *last* armed tick survives the typing storm; all earlier ticks are ignored. `tea.Tick` fires once per call, so N keystrokes arm N ticks but N−1 are dropped by the guard.\n\n2. **Cancel in-flight:** `m.cancel()` (from `context.WithCancel`) is called on every new keystroke and every `launch`. This stops wasted LLM/network work; the engine goroutine sees `ctx.Done()` and stops feeding the channel. `m.inflight` is set to `0` immediately so any late token is already unrenderable.\n\n3. **Stale-stream drop:** `launch` records `m.inflight = m.seq`. Every `streamEvent`/`doneMsg`/`errMsg` carries the `seq` it was born with; the handler drops anything where `msg.seq != m.inflight`. So even if a cancelled goroutine emits a final chunk before it notices `ctx.Done()`, it lands with a stale seq and is discarded in O(1) — **stale results can never render**. `ctx` cancellation is an optimization; the seq guard is the correctness guarantee.\n\nThe old `stream` channel from a superseded request is simply abandoned (its `waitStream` Cmd returns a `streamEvent` with a stale seq → dropped; the channel is GC'd when the goroutine closes it).\n\n## 6. Keybindings + help/footer (`keys.go`)\n\n```go\ntype keyMap struct {\n    Translate  key.Binding  // enter\n    ToggleLive key.Binding  // ctrl+l\n    CycleEngine key.Binding // ctrl+e   (copilot -> ollama -> google -> auto)\n    SwapLang   key.Binding  // ctrl+s   (source <-> target)\n    PickTarget key.Binding  // ctrl+t   (open target-language picker)\n    History    key.Binding  // ctrl+r   (recall / history picker)\n    Copy       key.Binding  // ctrl+y   (copy translation to clipboard)\n    Newline    key.Binding  // alt+enter\n    Confirm    key.Binding  // enter (in history)\n    Cancel     key.Binding  // esc\n    Quit       key.Binding  // ctrl+c / esc(at input-empty)\n}\n\nfunc defaultKeys() keyMap {\n    return keyMap{\n        Translate:  key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys(\"enter\"),      key.WithHelp(\"↵\", \"translate\")),\n        ToggleLive: key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys(\"ctrl+l\"),     key.WithHelp(\"^l\", \"live\")),\n        CycleEngine:key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys(\"ctrl+e\"),     key.WithHelp(\"^e\", \"engine\")),\n        SwapLang:   key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys(\"ctrl+s\"),     key.WithHelp(\"^s\", \"swap\")),\n        PickTarget: key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys(\"ctrl+t\"),     key.WithHelp(\"^t\", \"target\")),\n        History:    key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys(\"ctrl+r\"),     key.WithHelp(\"^r\", \"history\")),\n        Copy:       key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys(\"ctrl+y\"),     key.WithHelp(\"^y\", \"copy\")),\n        Quit:       key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys(\"ctrl+c\"),     key.WithHelp(\"^c\", \"quit\")),\n        // ...\n    }\n}\n\n// help.Model bindings\nfunc (k keyMap) ShortHelp() []key.Binding {\n    return []key.Binding{k.Translate, k.ToggleLive, k.CycleEngine, k.SwapLang, k.PickTarget, k.History, k.Copy, k.Quit}\n}\nfunc (k keyMap) FullHelp() [][]key.Binding { /* grouped rows */ }\n```\n\nFooter renders live state + help:\n```\n auto→es · claude-sonnet-5 (copilot) · live●  │  ↵ translate  ^l live  ^e engine  ^s swap  ^t target  ^r history  ^y copy  ^c quit\n```\n`live●` green when `debounceOn`, dim `live○` when off; engine segment shows forced engine or `auto`; a spinner replaces the status dot while `statTranslating`.\n\n## 7. Lip Gloss two-pane responsive layout (`view.go` + `relayout`)\n\n```\n┌────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────┐\n│ INPUT (textarea, h≈4)                   │ HISTORY (list)   │\n│ > Hola, ¿cómo estás?                    │  • hola…  en→es  │\n├────────────────────────────────────────┤  • good…  en→fr  │\n│ RESULT (viewport, scrollable)           │  • …             │\n│  Hello, how are you?                    │                  │\n│  ~ alt: Hi, how are you doing?          │  (ctrl+r focus)  │\n│  ⓘ register: informal · conf 0.94       │                  │\n├────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────┤\n│ auto→es · claude-sonnet-5 (copilot) · live●  │  ↵ … ^c quit│\n└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n```go\nfunc (m *Model) relayout() {\n    footerH := 1\n    bodyH   := m.height - footerH\n    // history pane hidden below a width threshold -> single column\n    var leftW, rightW int\n    if m.width >= 90 {\n        leftW  = m.width * 2 / 3\n        rightW = m.width - leftW\n    } else {\n        leftW, rightW = m.width, 0        // history becomes an overlay via showHistory\n    }\n    inputH := 4\n    m.ta.SetWidth(leftW - 2);  m.ta.SetHeight(inputH)\n    m.vp.SetWidth(leftW - 2);  m.vp.SetHeight(bodyH - inputH - 3) // borders\n    if rightW > 0 { m.hist.SetSize(rightW-2, bodyH) }\n}\n\nfunc (m Model) View() tea.View {\n    if !m.ready { return tea.NewView(\"initializing…\") }\n\n    left := lg.JoinVertical(lg.Left,\n        m.inputStyle().Render(m.ta.View()),\n        m.resultStyle().Render(m.vp.View()),\n    )\n\n    var body string\n    if m.width >= 90 {\n        right := m.paneStyle(m.focus == focusHistory).Width(m.rightW).Render(m.hist.View())\n        body = lg.JoinHorizontal(lg.Top, left, right)\n    } else if m.showHistory {\n        // overlay: render history centered over body\n        body = lg.Place(m.width, m.height-1, lg.Center, lg.Center, m.hist.View())\n    } else {\n        body = left\n    }\n\n    footer := m.footerStyle().Render(m.statusLine() + \"  │  \" + m.help.View(m.keys))\n    return tea.NewView(lg.JoinVertical(lg.Left, body, footer))\n}\n```\n\nStyling notes: no global renderer in v2 — build a `styles` struct from `applyTheme(isDark)` driven by `tea.BackgroundColorMsg`. Border highlight on the focused pane (`m.focus`). Streaming render (`renderStreaming`) shows raw buffer + a trailing spinner glyph; final render (`renderResult`) formats the structured `TranslateResult` (translation bold, `~ alt:` dim list, `ⓘ` notes/register, `conf` badge colored by `Confidence`).\n\n## 8. One-shot CLI path — shares engine, bypasses Bubble Tea (`cmd/root.go`)\n\n```go\nfunc run(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {\n    cfg, _ := config.Load()\n    st,  _ := store.Open(cfg)\n    chain  := engine.NewChain(cfg)          // SAME layer the TUI uses\n    ctx    := cmd.Context()\n\n    src, tgt := resolvePair(cfg, flagSource, flagTarget)  // lang.Fuzzy(\"chinees\")->zh\n\n    switch {\n    case len(args) > 0:                                   // translate \"text\"\n        return oneShot(ctx, chain, st, args, src, tgt)\n\n    case !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())):            // echo x | translate\n        b, _ := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)\n        return oneShot(ctx, chain, st, []string{strings.TrimSpace(string(b))}, src, tgt)\n\n    default:                                              // bare + TTY -> TUI\n        p := tea.NewProgram(tui.New(chain, st, cfg), tea.WithAltScreen())\n        _, err := p.Run()\n        return err\n    }\n}\n\nfunc oneShot(ctx context.Context, ch *engine.Chain, st store.Store,\n             args []string, src, tgt string) error {\n    text := strings.Join(args, \" \")\n    req  := engine.Request{Text: text, Source: src, Target: tgt, Interpret: true}\n\n    // stream to stdout only if stdout is a TTY (keep pipes clean)\n    var onTok func(string)\n    if term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd())) && !flagJSON {\n        onTok = func(t string) { fmt.Print(t) }\n    }\n    res, err := ch.Translate(ctx, req, onTok)\n    if err != nil { return err }\n\n    _ = st.Add(toEntry(res))\n    switch {\n    case flagJSON:                                        // --json: raw TranslateResult\n        json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout).Encode(res)\n    case onTok != nil:                                    // already streamed; newline + extras\n        fmt.Println()\n    default:                                              // piped: plain translation, no ANSI\n        fmt.Println(res.Translation)\n    }\n    return nil\n}\n```\n\nShared-layer guarantees:\n- `engine.Chain`, `store.Store`, `config`, `lang` are **identical objects** in both modes — the only fork is presentation (`fmt.Print` vs `vp.SetContent`) and whether Bubble Tea owns the TTY.\n- Color/ANSI gated on `stdout` being a TTY (Lip Gloss v2 auto-downsamples; plain path emits bare text so `translate x | pbcopy` is clean).\n- `--json` flag surfaces the full Marvin-lite struct for scripting; default piped output is `res.Translation` only.\n- cobra `SuggestionsMinimumDistance` (default) covers subcommand typos (\"did you mean\"); language-name fuzz is `lang.Fuzzy`, independent of cobra.\n\n## 9. `translate init` (huh standalone, not embedded)\n\nRun **outside** Bubble Tea (`form.Run()` blocks). Probe providers first, feed results into option descriptions:\n\n```go\nfunc runInit(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {\n    probes := engine.ProbeAll(cmd.Context())  // copilot:4141, ollama:11434, google\n    var engineChoice, model, target string\n    form := huh.NewForm(\n        huh.NewGroup(\n            huh.NewSelect[string]().Title(\"Default engine\").\n                Options(\n                    huh.NewOption(\"Auto fallback chain\", \"auto\"),\n                    huh.NewOption(probeLabel(\"copilot-proxy\", probes.Copilot), \"copilot\"),\n                    huh.NewOption(probeLabel(\"Ollama (offline)\", probes.Ollama), \"ollama\"),\n                    huh.NewOption(probeLabel(\"Google free\", probes.Google), \"google\"),\n                ).Value(&engineChoice),\n            huh.NewSelect[string]().Title(\"Model\").\n                OptionsFunc(func() []huh.Option[string] { return modelsFor(engineChoice, probes) }, &engineChoice).\n                Value(&model),\n            huh.NewInput().Title(\"Default target language\").Value(&target).\n                Validate(lang.ValidateFuzzy),   // accepts \"chinees\" -> zh\n        ),\n    )\n    if err := form.Run(); err != nil { return err }\n    return config.Save(config.FromWizard(engineChoice, model, target, probes))\n}\n```\n`probeLabel` appends `● up` / `○ down (start it with …)`; MODEL-ID GOTCHA handled in `modelsFor` (hyphenated ids from `GET /v1/models`, strip `[1m]`, reject dated ids; default `claude-sonnet-5`, fast `claude-haiku-4-5`/`gpt-5.4-mini`, max `claude-opus-4-8`, offline `llama3.2:3b`).\n\n## 10. Config / XDG / state (supporting the interaction layer)\n\n- **XDG resolver** (10-line, all platforms → `~/.config`, overriding adrg/stdlib macOS defaults): `configHome()`/`dataHome()`/`stateHome()` honor `XDG_*` env then fall back to `~/.config`, `~/.local/share`, `~/.local/state`. `EnsureDirs` + `MkdirAll` on first run.\n- `config.toml` via `go-toml/v2`; `[[provider]]` arrays-of-tables for the engine chain (`name`, `base_url`, `api_key_env`, `model`, `type`).\n- `state.json` under `XDG_STATE_HOME`: `{last_source, last_target, debounce_on}` — written on TUI exit and after each one-shot, read on startup so the last language pair + live-mode preference persist. **Not** in the DB.\n- **Store**: `Store` interface (`Add(Entry)`, `Search(q) []Entry`, `Recent(n) []Entry`, `LastPair()`), JSONL impl at `~/.local/share/translate/history.jsonl` + in-memory `sahilm/fuzzy`; modernc+FTS5 sqlite is a drop-in behind the same interface (locked decision preserved as an upgrade, not a v1 blocker).\n\n---\n\n### Files this design maps to (all absolute)\n`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/tui/{model,update,view,msgs,keys,styles}.go`, `.../internal/engine/{engine,chain,llm,google,mymemory,dict,detect,prompt}.go`, `.../internal/{config,lang,store}/…`, `.../cmd/{root,init}.go`, `.../main.go`.\n\n### Design invariants to preserve during implementation\n1. **`m.seq` is the only correctness mechanism** for debounce-collapse + stale-drop; `context` cancel is a work-saving optimization layered on top.\n2. **One channel, one reader (`waitStream`), one seq** — never spawn a second coordinating goroutine.\n3. **Engine layer imports zero Bubble Tea**; CLI and TUI diverge only at presentation.\n4. **Enter=translate, Alt+Enter=newline** so live-debounce + immediate-translate coexist.\n5. `View()` returns `tea.NewView(...)`; keys are `tea.KeyPressMsg`; window req is `tea.RequestWindowSize` (v2, `charm.land/*`).",
13	      "The directory is empty (greenfield, no `go.mod` yet). All decisions are locked and the research is thorough, so no clarifying questions are needed. Here is the engine/provider design.\n\n---\n\n# ENGINE / Provider Abstraction — Design\n\nPackage layout (all under module root, e.g. `module translate`):\n\n```\ninternal/engine/\n  engine.go     // interface, Request/Result/Chunk types, Mode/errors, registry helpers\n  llm.go        // OpenAI-compatible engine (copilot-proxy/Ollama/OpenRouter/LiteLLM/generic)\n  google.go     // free Google unofficial endpoint + MyMemory fallback\n  dict.go       // dictionaryapi.dev exact -> local Levenshtein fuzzy\n  chain.go      // AUTO fallback router (probe + per-request failover)\n  prompt.go     // built-in prompt templates + JSON schema\n  models.go     // model recommendation table + id normalization (copilot gotcha)\ninternal/lang/\n  resolve.go    // fuzzy language name/code resolver (\"chinees\"->zh), whatlanggo detect\n```\n\n## 1. Core types and the `Engine` interface (`engine.go`)\n\n```go\npackage engine\n\nimport \"context\"\n\ntype Mode int\nconst (\n    ModeTranslate Mode = iota // register-aware faithful translation\n    ModeDict                  // dictionary lookup (definitions)\n)\n\n// Request is the single input for every engine. Source==\"\" or \"auto\" => detect.\ntype Request struct {\n    Text    string\n    Source  string // BCP-47-ish code or \"auto\"\n    Target  string // e.g. \"zh\", \"es\"\n    Mode    Mode\n    MaxAlts int    // cap on Alternatives (default 3); 0 => engine default\n    Stream  bool   // caller wants token streaming (LLM only; others ignore)\n}\n\n// TranslateResult is the \"Marvin-lite\" typed result. Every engine fills what it can;\n// zero-value fields are simply absent. This is also the history/JSONL record shape.\ntype TranslateResult struct {\n    Translation    string       `json:\"translation\"`\n    DetectedSource string       `json:\"detected_source,omitempty\"`\n    Target         string       `json:\"target\"`\n    Alternatives   []string     `json:\"alternatives,omitempty\"`\n    Notes          string       `json:\"notes,omitempty\"`\n    Confidence     float64      `json:\"confidence,omitempty\"` // 0..1, best-effort\n    // Dictionary payload (Mode==ModeDict). Nil for translate mode.\n    Dictionary     *DictEntry   `json:\"dictionary,omitempty\"`\n    // Provenance — filled by the chain, not by the engine itself.\n    Engine         string       `json:\"engine,omitempty\"`\n    Model          string       `json:\"model,omitempty\"`\n    Fuzzy          bool         `json:\"fuzzy,omitempty\"` // dict fell back to nearest headword\n    FuzzyMatched   string       `json:\"fuzzy_matched,omitempty\"`\n}\n\ntype DictEntry struct {\n    Word      string    `json:\"word\"`\n    Phonetic  string    `json:\"phonetic,omitempty\"`\n    Meanings  []Meaning `json:\"meanings\"`\n    SourceURL string    `json:\"source_url,omitempty\"`\n}\ntype Meaning struct {\n    PartOfSpeech string       `json:\"part_of_speech\"`\n    Definitions  []Definition `json:\"definitions\"`\n    Synonyms     []string     `json:\"synonyms,omitempty\"`\n    Antonyms     []string     `json:\"antonyms,omitempty\"`\n}\ntype Definition struct {\n    Text     string `json:\"definition\"`\n    Example  string `json:\"example,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// Chunk is one streamed unit. Kind distinguishes live tokens from the terminal result.\ntype Chunk struct {\n    Kind   ChunkKind\n    Text   string           // token text (Kind==ChunkToken)\n    Result *TranslateResult // final structured result (Kind==ChunkDone)\n    Err    error            // Kind==ChunkError (also closes the stream)\n}\ntype ChunkKind int\nconst (\n    ChunkToken ChunkKind = iota\n    ChunkDone\n    ChunkError\n)\n\n// Engine is the provider abstraction. All methods honor ctx cancellation.\ntype Engine interface {\n    Name() string\n    // Translate runs one request. Implementations return a receive-only channel and\n    // MUST close it after sending exactly one terminal Chunk (ChunkDone or ChunkError).\n    // Streaming engines emit >=0 ChunkToken then one ChunkDone. Non-streaming engines\n    // emit a single ChunkDone. Cancelling ctx stops work and closes the channel.\n    Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error)\n    // Detect returns a best-effort source language code (offline-cheap; may be \"\").\n    Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error)\n    // Available is a cheap health probe (TCP/HTTP HEAD/models list) used by the router.\n    Available(ctx context.Context) bool\n    // Supports lets the router skip engines that can't serve a mode (e.g. Google can't dict).\n    Supports(m Mode) bool\n}\n```\n\nDesign notes:\n- **Channel-return, not callback.** The channel maps 1:1 onto the TUI's self-resubscribing `waitChunk` reader from the verified Bubble Tea pattern. The `seq` guard in the TUI drops stale chunks; the engine only needs to respect `ctx`. One-shot CLI just range-drains the channel and prints the terminal `ChunkDone.Result`.\n- **The synchronous `error` return** is only for immediate setup failures (bad request, nil client). All runtime/network errors flow as a terminal `ChunkError` so the caller has one drain path.\n- **Terminal-chunk contract** (exactly one `ChunkDone` or `ChunkError`, then close) makes `waitChunk`'s \"channel closed => done\" logic unambiguous and lets non-streaming engines share the exact same shape.\n\nHelper for non-streaming engines (google, dict) so they don't each reimplement the channel dance:\n\n```go\nfunc single(res *TranslateResult, err error) <-chan Chunk {\n    ch := make(chan Chunk, 1)\n    if err != nil { ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err} } else { ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res} }\n    close(ch)\n    return ch\n}\n```\n\n## 2. LLM engine (`llm.go`)\n\nOne struct parameterizes all OpenAI-compatible backends (copilot-proxy / Ollama / OpenRouter / LiteLLM / generic).\n\n```go\ntype LLMConfig struct {\n    Name       string // \"copilot\", \"ollama\", \"openrouter\", ...\n    BaseURL    string // e.g. \"http://localhost:4141/v1\"\n    Model      string // e.g. \"claude-sonnet-5\"\n    APIKeyEnv  string // env var name; \"\" => no Authorization header (copilot-proxy)\n    JSONSchema bool   // whether to try response_format=json_schema (true for copilot/OpenRouter)\n    Timeout    time.Duration\n}\n\ntype LLMEngine struct {\n    cfg  LLMConfig\n    key  string        // resolved once from os.Getenv(APIKeyEnv); \"\" if unset/empty\n    http *http.Client\n}\n\nfunc NewLLM(cfg LLMConfig) *LLMEngine {\n    key := \"\"\n    if cfg.APIKeyEnv != \"\" { key = os.Getenv(cfg.APIKeyEnv) }\n    return &LLMEngine{cfg: cfg, key: key, http: &http.Client{Timeout: cfg.Timeout}}\n}\nfunc (e *LLMEngine) Name() string        { return e.cfg.Name }\nfunc (e *LLMEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return true } // LLM can translate AND define\n```\n\n### Model-id normalization (copilot gotcha) — `models.go`\n\n```go\n// normalizeModelID: strip any \"[1m]\" (context-window) suffix, drop dated suffixes,\n// keep hyphenated canonical id. copilot-proxy lists e.g. \"claude-opus-4-8\" / \"gpt-5.4-mini\".\nfunc normalizeModelID(id string) string {\n    id = strings.TrimSpace(id)\n    if i := strings.Index(id, \"[\"); i >= 0 { id = strings.TrimSpace(id[:i]) } // strip \"[1m]\"\n    // drop trailing \"-YYYYMMDD\" or \"@date\" dated forms if present\n    id = datedSuffix.ReplaceAllString(id, \"\") // regexp: `(-\\d{8}|@\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2})$`\n    return id\n}\n```\nApply `normalizeModelID(e.cfg.Model)` when building the request body. Init/`translate init` calls `GET {BaseURL}/models`, runs each returned id through the same normalizer, and offers them in the wizard.\n\n### Availability probe\n\n```go\nfunc (e *LLMEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {\n    // cheap: GET {BaseURL}/models with a short (~800ms) sub-context.\n    ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 800*time.Millisecond); defer cancel()\n    req, _ := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, e.cfg.BaseURL+\"/models\", nil)\n    e.auth(req)\n    resp, err := e.http.Do(req)\n    if err != nil { return false }\n    defer resp.Body.Close()\n    return resp.StatusCode == 200\n}\nfunc (e *LLMEngine) auth(r *http.Request) {\n    if e.key != \"\" { r.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer \"+e.key) } // ONLY when key present\n}\n```\nCopilot-proxy sends no auth (`APIKeyEnv==\"\"` => `e.key==\"\"` => no header). OpenRouter sets `APIKeyEnv:\"OPENROUTER_API_KEY\"`.\n\n### Building messages + request body\n\n```go\nfunc (e *LLMEngine) buildBody(req Request, stream bool) chatRequest {\n    sys, user := buildPrompt(req)              // from prompt.go\n    body := chatRequest{\n        Model:    normalizeModelID(e.cfg.Model),\n        Messages: []chatMsg{{Role: \"system\", Content: sys}, {Role: \"user\", Content: user}},\n        Stream:   stream,\n    }\n    if e.cfg.JSONSchema {\n        body.ResponseFormat = jsonSchemaFormat(req.Mode) // response_format json_schema (prompt.go)\n    }\n    return body\n}\n```\n`response_format` is set only when `JSONSchema` is true; otherwise we rely on the prompt's \"reply with only JSON\" instruction plus robust extraction. Ollama and some LiteLLM configs ignore/500 on `json_schema`, hence the flag defaults false for those and true for copilot/OpenRouter.\n\n### Translate: streaming + non-streaming, both yielding a structured result\n\n```go\nfunc (e *LLMEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {\n    if strings.TrimSpace(req.Text) == \"\" { return nil, ErrEmptyInput }\n    stream := req.Stream\n    ch := make(chan Chunk, 32) // buffer so fast SSE doesn't block on render frames\n    go func() {\n        defer close(ch)\n        body := e.buildBody(req, stream)\n        httpReq := e.newChatRequest(ctx, body) // POST {BaseURL}/chat/completions, auth()\n        resp, err := e.http.Do(httpReq)\n        if err != nil { ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}; return }\n        defer resp.Body.Close()\n        if resp.StatusCode != 200 {\n            ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: httpErr(resp)}; return\n        }\n        var full strings.Builder\n        if stream {\n            // SSE parse: lines \"data: {json}\\n\\n\"; final \"data: [DONE]\".\n            sc := bufio.NewScanner(resp.Body)\n            sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64*1024), 1<<20)\n            for sc.Scan() {\n                line := sc.Text()\n                if !strings.HasPrefix(line, \"data:\") { continue }\n                payload := strings.TrimSpace(line[5:])\n                if payload == \"[DONE]\" { break }\n                var d streamDelta\n                if json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &d) != nil { continue }\n                tok := d.Choices[0].Delta.Content\n                if tok == \"\" { continue }\n                full.WriteString(tok)\n                select {\n                case ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkToken, Text: tok}:\n                case <-ctx.Done(): ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: ctx.Err()}; return\n                }\n            }\n            if err := sc.Err(); err != nil { ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}; return }\n        } else {\n            var cr chatResponse\n            if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&cr); err != nil {\n                ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}; return\n            }\n            full.WriteString(cr.Choices[0].Message.Content)\n        }\n        // Parse the accumulated content into TranslateResult (works for both paths).\n        res, perr := parseLLMResult(full.String(), req)\n        if perr != nil { ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: perr}; return }\n        res.Engine, res.Model = e.cfg.Name, body.Model\n        ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}\n    }()\n    return ch, nil\n}\n```\n\nKey point: **live tokens stream to the UI for perceived latency, but the authoritative structured `TranslateResult` is parsed from the fully-accumulated content at the end.** During streaming the TUI shows raw tokens (or the incrementally-visible `translation` field); on `ChunkDone` it swaps in the structured render (alternatives/notes/confidence). The token stream is the model emitting JSON, so the live view is \"JSON being typed\" — acceptable; optionally the TUI can show only the growing `\"translation\"` value by a cheap incremental scan, but that's a UI concern, not the engine's.\n\n### Robust structured-output parsing\n\n```go\nfunc parseLLMResult(content string, req Request) (*TranslateResult, error) {\n    raw := extractJSON(content) // 1) whole string if it parses; else 2) fenced ```json block;\n                                // 3) first balanced {...} span. Returns \"\" if none.\n    if raw != \"\" {\n        var r TranslateResult\n        if json.Unmarshal([]byte(raw), &r) == nil && r.Translation != \"\" {\n            if r.Target == \"\" { r.Target = req.Target }\n            if req.MaxAlts > 0 && len(r.Alternatives) > req.MaxAlts {\n                r.Alternatives = r.Alternatives[:req.MaxAlts]\n            }\n            return &r, nil\n        }\n    }\n    // Fallback: model ignored the schema — treat the (stripped) reply as plain translation.\n    return &TranslateResult{\n        Translation: strings.TrimSpace(stripFences(content)),\n        Target:      req.Target,\n    }, nil\n}\n```\n`extractJSON` handles the three common model behaviors (clean JSON, fenced JSON, prose-then-JSON) with a brace-balance scanner that respects string literals/escapes. This makes the engine resilient whether or not `response_format` was honored — Ollama/llama3.2 especially will need it. `Detect` on the LLM engine can reuse a tiny prompt or just defer to the offline detector; simplest is to return `lang.DetectOffline(text)` to avoid a round-trip.\n\n## 3. Free Google engine (`google.go`)\n\n```go\ntype GoogleEngine struct{ http *http.Client }\nfunc (GoogleEngine) Name() string { return \"google\" }\nfunc (GoogleEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeTranslate }\nfunc (e GoogleEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool { return probeGET(ctx, e.http, googleHealthURL) }\n```\n\nRequest: `GET https://translate.googleapis.com/translate_a/single?client=gtx&sl={src}&tl={tgt}&dt=t&dt=bd&dt=at&q={urlenc}` with a browser-ish `User-Agent`.\n\nParsing (positions verified):\n```go\n// data is []json.RawMessage-ish nested arrays; decode into []interface{} and walk.\n// - Translation: concat data[0][i][0] over all segments i (long text splits!).\n// - DetectedSource: data[2] (string). Confidence: data[8][2][0] (float) if present.\n// - Alternatives: pull from data[5] (alt translations) and/or data[1] (bilingual dict block,\n//   populated only for single lemmas), dedup, cap at req.MaxAlts.\n```\nOn any non-200 (notably **429** after bursts) return a `ChunkError` so the chain fails over. Because the TUI's 400ms debounce + cancel-in-flight already throttles to one request per pause, no client-side rate limiter is needed in v1; add exponential backoff+jitter on 429 only if it bites. `Detect` = same call with `dt=t` and read `data[2]`, or offline `whatlanggo` when offline.\n\n**MyMemory secondary** (trivial flat shape) lives behind the same engine type or as a sibling `MyMemoryEngine`; the chain can list `google` then `mymemory`. MyMemory needs an explicit langpair (no auto-detect), so when `Source==\"auto\"` run `lang.DetectOffline` first. Keep it optional/config-gated for v1.\n\n## 4. Dictionary engine (`dict.go`)\n\n```go\ntype DictEngine struct {\n    http     *http.Client\n    wordlist *fuzzyIndex // bundled SCOWL/words, lazy-loaded; nearest-headword via Levenshtein\n}\nfunc (DictEngine) Name() string { return \"dictionary\" }\nfunc (DictEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeDict }\n```\n\nLookup, exact -> fuzzy:\n```go\nfunc (e *DictEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {\n    word := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(req.Text))\n    entry, status, err := e.fetch(ctx, word) // GET api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries/en/{word}\n    if err == nil && status == 200 {\n        return single(&TranslateResult{Dictionary: entry, Translation: entry.gloss(), Target: req.Target}, nil), nil\n    }\n    if status == 404 { // API gives NO suggestions -> resolve nearest headword locally\n        if cand, ok := e.wordlist.Nearest(word, 2 /*max edit distance*/); ok {\n            if entry2, s2, e2 := e.fetch(ctx, cand); e2 == nil && s2 == 200 {\n                r := &TranslateResult{Dictionary: entry2, Translation: entry2.gloss(),\n                    Target: req.Target, Fuzzy: true, FuzzyMatched: cand}\n                return single(r, nil), nil\n            }\n        }\n        return single(nil, ErrNoDictEntry), nil\n    }\n    return single(nil, err), nil\n}\n```\n`wordlist.Nearest` = prefix/trigram pre-filter then `github.com/agnivade/levenshtein` ranking (Damerau-friendly), mirroring cobra's \"did you mean\" UX and keeping fuzzy offline-deterministic. `Available` = probe dictionaryapi.dev (or always true if wordlist bundled, since fuzzy works offline once a network entry is cached — but for v1 probe the API).\n\n## 5. AUTO fallback router (`chain.go`)\n\n```go\ntype Chain struct {\n    engines   []Engine       // in configured priority order\n    ttl       time.Duration  // availability cache TTL (~5s)\n    mu        sync.Mutex\n    avail     map[string]probeCache\n}\ntype probeCache struct { ok bool; at time.Time }\n\nfunc NewChain(engines []Engine, ttl time.Duration) *Chain { ... }\nfunc (c *Chain) Name() string { return \"auto\" }\nfunc (c *Chain) Supports(m Mode) bool { for _, e := range c.engines { if e.Supports(m) { return true } }; return false }\n```\n\nCached probe (avoids a health check on every keystroke):\n```go\nfunc (c *Chain) available(ctx context.Context, e Engine) bool {\n    c.mu.Lock()\n    if pc, ok := c.avail[e.Name()]; ok && time.Since(pc.at) < c.ttl {\n        c.mu.Unlock(); return pc.ok\n    }\n    c.mu.Unlock()\n    ok := e.Available(ctx)\n    c.mu.Lock(); c.avail[e.Name()] = probeCache{ok, time.Now()}; c.mu.Unlock()\n    return ok\n}\n```\n\nPer-request selection + failover. The router returns a proxy channel so it can transparently fail over to the next engine if the chosen one emits `ChunkError` **before** producing any token (once tokens have streamed to the UI we don't silently switch — see note):\n```go\nfunc (c *Chain) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {\n    candidates := c.orderFor(req.Mode) // filter Supports(req.Mode), keep priority order\n    if len(candidates) == 0 { return nil, ErrNoEngineForMode }\n    out := make(chan Chunk, 32)\n    go func() {\n        defer close(out)\n        var lastErr error\n        for _, e := range candidates {\n            if !c.available(ctx, e) { continue }         // skip down engines cheaply\n            sub, err := e.Translate(ctx, req)\n            if err != nil { lastErr = err; continue }     // setup failure -> next engine\n            streamed := false\n            fail := false\n            for ch := range sub {\n                switch ch.Kind {\n                case ChunkToken:\n                    streamed = true\n                    out <- ch\n                case ChunkDone:\n                    out <- ch                             // success (attach provenance already set)\n                    return\n                case ChunkError:\n                    lastErr = ch.Err\n                    c.markDown(e)                         // demote in avail cache immediately\n                    fail = true\n                }\n            }\n            if fail && !streamed { continue }             // clean failover: nothing shown yet\n            if fail && streamed {                         // already streamed partial -> surface error, don't restart\n                out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: lastErr}; return\n            }\n        }\n        out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf(\"all engines failed: %w\", lastErr)}\n    }()\n    return out, nil\n}\n```\nDesign decisions:\n- **Probe order = configured order** (`copilot -> ollama -> google` by default), filtered by `Supports(Mode)` — so `ModeDict` naturally routes to the dictionary engine regardless of translate priority.\n- **Availability cached ~5s** keyed by engine name; a keystroke storm reuses the probe. `markDown` sets `ok=false` immediately on a runtime error so the next debounce cycle skips it without waiting for TTL.\n- **Failover only before first token.** Once tokens have been shown live in the TUI, silently swapping engines would garble the pane; instead we surface the error. Pre-token failures (connection refused, non-200, empty-model list) switch cleanly — this covers the common \"copilot-proxy not running -> Ollama\" case since that fails at connect/`Available` time, never mid-stream.\n- `Available()` on the chain = \"any candidate available\"; `Detect` = first available engine's detect, else `lang.DetectOffline`.\n\nConfig → chain construction (from `config.toml`, `[[provider]]` array-of-tables via go-toml/v2). `engine = \"auto\"` builds the default `copilot,ollama,google` chain; a named engine (`engine=\"copilot\"`) builds a single-element chain (still gets the uniform channel/failover-to-error semantics).\n\n## 6. Built-in prompt templates (`prompt.go`) — actual text\n\n```go\nconst translateSystemPrompt = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.\nTranslate the user's text into the target language faithfully and idiomatically.\nRules:\n- Preserve meaning, tone, and register (formal/informal, technical/casual) of the source.\n- The user may have typos, slang, or ungrammatical input. Interpret their intended meaning\n  and translate that intended meaning; do not translate the typo literally and do not refuse.\n- Do NOT add commentary, greetings, or explanations outside the JSON.\n- If the source language is given as \"auto\", detect it and report it.\n- Provide up to %d distinct alternative renderings when meaningfully different (else fewer).\n- Keep \"notes\" to at most one short sentence: only for genuine ambiguity, idiom, or a\n  register choice worth flagging. Leave it empty when the translation is straightforward.\n- \"confidence\" is your 0.0-1.0 self-estimate of translation fidelity.\nRespond with ONLY a JSON object matching this schema, no markdown fences:\n{\n  \"translation\": string,            // the primary translation, target language\n  \"detected_source\": string,        // BCP-47-ish code, e.g. \"en\", \"zh\", \"es\"\n  \"target\": string,                 // echo the target code\n  \"alternatives\": [string],         // 0..%d alternates, most natural first\n  \"notes\": string,                  // brief; \"\" if none\n  \"confidence\": number              // 0.0 - 1.0\n}`\n\nconst translateUserPrompt = `Source language: %s\nTarget language: %s\nText to translate:\n\"\"\"\n%s\n\"\"\"`\n```\nBuild:\n```go\nfunc buildPrompt(req Request) (system, user string) {\n    n := req.MaxAlts; if n <= 0 { n = 3 }\n    src := req.Source; if src == \"\" { src = \"auto\" }\n    system = fmt.Sprintf(translateSystemPrompt, n, n)\n    user   = fmt.Sprintf(translateUserPrompt, src, req.Target, req.Text)\n    return\n}\n```\nDict-mode LLM prompt (fallback when the dictionary API is down and Mode==ModeDict routes to LLM) mirrors the `DictEntry` schema; omitted here for brevity but same pattern.\n\nJSON-schema for `response_format` (copilot/OpenRouter path) — `strict:true`, `additionalProperties:false`, required `[\"translation\",\"target\"]`:\n```go\nfunc jsonSchemaFormat(m Mode) *responseFormat {\n    return &responseFormat{Type: \"json_schema\", JSONSchema: schemaDef{\n        Name: \"translate_result\", Strict: true, Schema: translateResultSchema, // marshaled JSON Schema\n    }}\n}\n```\n\n## 7. Model recommendation table (`models.go`, surfaced to `init` + `--help`)\n\nPresented in the `translate init` wizard (as huh `Select` options with descriptions) and stored as `model` in config. Ids are already normalized (hyphenated, no `[1m]`, no dated ids).\n\n| Role | Model id | When to use |\n|------|----------|-------------|\n| **Default** | `claude-sonnet-5` | Best balance of quality/speed for everyday translation. |\n| **Fast** | `claude-haiku-4-5` (alt `gpt-5.4-mini`) | Low latency, live-debounce typing, short text. |\n| **Max** | `claude-opus-4-8` | Hardest passages, literary/technical nuance, long text. |\n| **Offline** | `llama3.2:3b` (Ollama) | No network / copilot-proxy down; only pulled Ollama model. |\n\nRepresented as:\n```go\ntype ModelRec struct { Role, ID, Note string }\nvar recommended = []ModelRec{\n    {\"default\", \"claude-sonnet-5\",  \"balanced quality/speed\"},\n    {\"fast\",    \"claude-haiku-4-5\", \"low latency; gpt-5.4-mini alt\"},\n    {\"max\",     \"claude-opus-4-8\",  \"highest quality\"},\n    {\"offline\", \"llama3.2:3b\",      \"Ollama, no network\"},\n}\n```\n`init` probes each provider (`GET /models`, TCP to Ollama :11434, HTTP to googleapis) and only offers models actually served; unavailable ones render greyed with a \"not detected\" description.\n\n## Caveat to surface in README / `init`\nCopilot ToS discourages non-GitHub agents hitting copilot-proxy; note it at first run and make the engine order trivially reconfigurable (drop copilot, lead with Ollama/Google) via `config.toml`.\n\n---\n\n### Cross-cutting contracts (for implementers)\n- Every engine's `Translate` returns a channel that closes after exactly one terminal chunk; callers (TUI `waitChunk`, CLI drain) rely on close == done.\n- Runtime errors are terminal `ChunkError`s (not the synchronous return), giving one uniform failover/drain path.\n- `ctx` cancellation is the sole stop signal; the TUI's `seq` guard makes cancelled results unrenderable, so engines need not know about staleness.\n- Provenance (`Engine`, `Model`, `Fuzzy`) is stamped by the producing engine/chain and persisted verbatim into the JSONL history record (same `TranslateResult` struct), keeping history a drop-in for the future sqlite/FTS5 `Store` swap.\n\nRelevant target files (none created yet — greenfield): `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/engine/{engine,llm,google,dict,chain,prompt,models}.go` and `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/lang/{resolve}.go`.",
14	      "The design is complete and written to the plan file. Returning it as my response.\n\n# translate — CONFIG + STORAGE + CLI-command layer (design)\n\nGrounded on this host's `mi-router` precedence idiom (flags > config.toml > env > interactive-prompt-with-save) and the repo's Television channel `.toml` shape (`[source] command/display/output/no_sort`, `[preview]`, `[keybindings]`, `[actions.*]` with `{split:\\t:N}`, `mode = fork|execute`, clipboard via the repo's `x` tool). Go is 1.26.4, so the Charm v2 `charm.land/*` stack (Go 1.25 floor) is fine.\n\nEngines/TUI are sibling layers; this design defines the seams they plug into (config shape, `Store`, `TranslateResult`, lang resolver, state file) without implementing them.\n\nFull design file: `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/.claude/plans/cli-tui-rosy-alpaca-agent-a4fd8d5dd7034165c.md`\n\n## 0. Package layout\n\n```\ntranslate/\n  main.go                      # cobra Execute()\n  internal/\n    xdgpath/   paths.go        # ~/.config, ~/.local/share, ~/.local/state resolver\n    config/    config.go resolve.go   # structs, Default/Load/Save/Merge; flags>file>env resolver\n    state/     state.go        # last pair / source-mode persistence\n    store/     store.go jsonl.go sqlite.go   # Store iface + v1 jsonl + upgrade sqlite\n    lang/      lang.go         # ISO-639 table + fuzzy Resolve()\n    engine/    engine.go result.go prompt/   # SEAM: Engine, Request, Chain, TranslateResult, prompts\n    tui/       ...             # Bubble Tea (sibling)\n  cmd/         root once init history search config providers lang completion\n```\n\nDeps: `spf13/cobra`; `charm.land/{bubbletea,bubbles,lipgloss,huh}/v2`; `pelletier/go-toml/v2`; `sahilm/fuzzy`; `agnivade/levenshtein`; `abadojack/whatlanggo`; `oklog/ulid/v2`; `golang.org/x/term`; `modernc.org/sqlite` (upgrade only).\n\n## 1. XDG paths (`internal/xdgpath`)\n\nDo **not** use `adrg/xdg` or `os.UserConfigDir()` — both resolve to `~/Library/Application Support` on macOS, breaking the `~/.config` convention. Roll the freedesktop resolver (honors `XDG_*`, else `~/.config` / `~/.local/share` / `~/.local/state` on macOS **and** Linux):\n\n```go\nfunc base(env, def string) string {\n    if v := os.Getenv(env); v != \"\" { return v }\n    h, _ := os.UserHomeDir(); return filepath.Join(h, def)\n}\nfunc ConfigDir() string { return filepath.Join(base(\"XDG_CONFIG_HOME\",\".config\"),\"translate\") }\nfunc DataDir()   string { return filepath.Join(base(\"XDG_DATA_HOME\",\".local/share\"),\"translate\") }\nfunc StateDir()  string { return filepath.Join(base(\"XDG_STATE_HOME\",\".local/state\"),\"translate\") }\n```\n\n`EnsureDirs()` = `MkdirAll(0o755)` on all three, called from a cobra `PersistentPreRunE`; missing config.toml → write `Default()` + hint to run `translate init` (skipped for `init`/`completion`).\n\n## 2. config.toml schema (`internal/config`)\n\n### 2.1 Go structs (go-toml/v2; `[[provider]]` = `[]Provider`)\n\n```go\ntype Config struct {\n    General   General; Chain Chain\n    Providers []Provider `toml:\"provider\"`   // array-of-tables\n    Google    Google; Dict Dict; History History\n}\ntype General struct {\n    DefaultTarget, DefaultSource string          // \"en\", \"auto\"|code\n    RememberLastPair, LiveTranslate, Stream bool\n    Engine, Tier, Color string                   // auto|llm|google|dict ; default|fast|max ; auto|always|never\n    DebounceMs, AlternativesCount int\n}\ntype Chain struct { Order []string }             // provider names + pseudo \"google\"/\"dict\"\ntype Provider struct {\n    Name, Type, BaseURL, Model string            // type: openai|ollama|openrouter|litellm|generic\n    ModelFast, ModelMax, APIKeyEnv string        // APIKeyEnv==\"\" => no Authorization header\n}\n// Google{Enabled,Endpoint,ExtraDT[],Fallback,UserAgent,TimeoutMs}\n// Dict{Enabled,Endpoint,Lang,Fuzzy,Wordlist,MaxSuggestions}\n// History{Enabled,Backend,Path,MaxEntries,Dedupe}   // backend: jsonl|sqlite\n```\n\n### 2.2 Example / `Default()` first-run file\n\n```toml\n[general]\ndefault_target = \"en\"\ndefault_source = \"auto\"          # \"auto\" = detect, or fixed code e.g. \"zh\"\nremember_last_pair = true\nengine = \"auto\"                  # auto | llm | google | dict\nlive_translate = true            # auto-translate ~debounce_ms after you stop typing\ndebounce_ms = 400\nalternatives_count = 3\ntier = \"default\"                 # default | fast | max\nstream = true\ncolor = \"auto\"\n\n[chain]\norder = [\"copilot\", \"ollama\", \"google\", \"dict\"]\n\n[[provider]]\nname = \"copilot\"                 # copilot-proxy: NO auth header\ntype = \"openai\"\nbase_url = \"http://localhost:4141/v1\"\nmodel      = \"claude-sonnet-5\"   # recommended default\nmodel_fast = \"claude-haiku-4-5\"  # gpt-5.4-mini also good\nmodel_max  = \"claude-opus-4-8\"\n# hyphenated ids only; strip \"[1m]\" suffix; never dated ids.\n# CAVEAT: Copilot ToS discourages non-GitHub agents.\n\n[[provider]]\nname = \"ollama\"\ntype = \"ollama\"\nbase_url = \"http://localhost:11434\"\nmodel = \"llama3.2:3b\"            # offline fallback\n\n[[provider]]\nname = \"openrouter\"\ntype = \"openrouter\"\nbase_url = \"https://openrouter.ai/api/v1\"\nmodel = \"anthropic/claude-sonnet-5\"\napi_key_env = \"OPENROUTER_API_KEY\"\n\n[google]\nenabled = true\nendpoint = \"https://translate.googleapis.com/translate_a/single\"\nextra_dt = [\"bd\", \"at\"]          # bilingual-dict + alternates slots\nfallback = \"mymemory\"            # \"\" | \"mymemory\" on 429/403\nuser_agent = \"Mozilla/5.0 translate-cli\"\ntimeout_ms = 4000\n\n[dict]\nenabled = true\nendpoint = \"https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries\"\nlang = \"en\"\nfuzzy = true                     # on 404, local Levenshtein nearest headword\nwordlist = \"\"                    # \"\" = bundled\nmax_suggestions = 5\n\n[history]\nenabled = true\nbackend = \"jsonl\"                # jsonl (v1) | sqlite (upgrade)\npath = \"\"                        # \"\" = XDG default\nmax_entries = 0\ndedupe = true\n```\n\n`Save()` = `toml.Marshal` (v2 round-trips `[[provider]]`) → temp file + `os.Rename`.\n\n## 3. Precedence (mi-router idiom) — `resolve.go`\n\nPer-setting, **first non-empty wins**:\n\n```\n1. CLI flag        (--engine, --to, --from, --provider, --model, --tier, --no-live …)\n2. config.toml\n3. env var         (TRANSLATE_ENGINE, TRANSLATE_TARGET, TRANSLATE_SOURCE,\n                    TRANSLATE_PROVIDER, TRANSLATE_MODEL, TRANSLATE_TIER, TRANSLATE_CONFIG)\n4. interactive prompt (TTY only — the init wizard; offers to save)\n```\n\nConfig ranks **above** env deliberately (mi-router semantics, unlike viper). Two env roles: *behavior* (`TRANSLATE_*`, in the chain) vs *secret* (named by `provider.api_key_env`, read at request time to build `Authorization: Bearer`, never stored). `Resolve(cfg, flags) Resolved` produces the merged view handed to engines/TUI; `config get <key>` prints resolved value + its source (flag/config/env/default).\n\n## 4. Last-pair state (`internal/state`)\n\n`XDG_STATE_HOME/translate/state.json`, rewritten atomically after each success and on TUI exit:\n\n```jsonc\n{ \"source\":\"auto\", \"target\":\"es\", \"source_mode\":\"auto\",   // auto|fixed\n  \"provider\":\"copilot\", \"engine\":\"auto\", \"updated_at\":\"2026-07-09T18:51:00Z\" }\n```\n\n**Bare `translate` restore:** on TUI start, if `remember_last_pair`, `state.Load()` seeds source/target/source_mode/provider (empty fields fall back to `general.default_*`); if false, always use config defaults. One-shot also honors state target/source when no `--to/--from` given, so `translate \"hola\"` continues the last pair; flags override and re-save.\n\n## 5. History store (`internal/store`)\n\nv1 backend = **JSONL append + in-memory fuzzy** (append-mostly, low-volume, zero schema). SQLite+FTS5 is a drop-in upgrade behind the same interface.\n\n```go\ntype Record struct {\n    ID string; TS time.Time\n    SourceLang, TargetLang, Engine, Model, Input, Output string\n    Alternatives []string; Notes string; Confidence float64; Favorite bool\n}\ntype Query struct { Text, Pair, Engine string; FavoritesOnly bool; Since time.Time; Limit int }\ntype Store interface {\n    Add(ctx, Record) (Record, error)      // assigns ULID/TS; applies dedupe\n    Search(ctx, Query) ([]Record, error)\n    Recent(ctx, limit int) ([]Record, error)\n    Get(ctx, id string) (Record, error)\n    SetFavorite(ctx, id string, fav bool) error\n    Close() error\n}\nfunc Open(cfg config.History) (Store, error)   // switch on Backend\n```\n\n- **JSONL:** `Add` = ULID+TS, optional dedupe on `Input|Pair|Engine`, `O_APPEND` one JSON line. `Search`/`Recent` read all lines once (µs for thousands), filter, then `sahilm/fuzzy`-rank `Input`/`Output` (or newest-first). `SetFavorite`/trim = atomic full-file rewrite (temp+rename) — semantics identical to sqlite's in-place UPDATE.\n- **SQLite (upgrade):** driver `modernc.org/sqlite` (name `\"sqlite\"`, cgo-free, FTS5 built in), DSN `file:…?_pragma=journal_mode(WAL)&_pragma=busy_timeout(5000)`. Self-test `SELECT sqlite_compileoption_used('ENABLE_FTS5')`; if 0, fall back to `LIKE`. Schema: base `history` table + `history_fts USING fts5(input,output,notes, content='history', content_rowid='rowid')` + INSERT/DELETE/UPDATE sync triggers; `Search` = `... history_fts MATCH ? ORDER BY rank`.\n- **Write point:** every *successful* translation (one-shot + TUI final `doneMsg`) builds a `Record` from `TranslateResult` + resolved engine/model/pair, calls `Add`, then `state.Save`. Failures not persisted.\n\n## 6. Machine output + `tv translate` channel\n\n`translate list --tsv` column contract (col 0 = id for tv preview/actions):\n\n```\n0 id(ULID)  1 ts(RFC3339)  2 pair(en>es)  3 engine  4 input(escaped 1-line)  5 output(escaped)  6 fav(★|·)\n```\n\n`history --tsv` reuses it; `--json` emits `[]Record`. Escaping `\\t\\n\\r` in cols 4/5 is load-bearing. Companion `translate show <id>` renders one record as markdown, or `--field output`/`--json` for a single field.\n\nChannel `dot_config/television/cable/translate.toml` (mirrors the repo's `yth.toml` idiom — `--tsv` source, `{split:\\t:N}`, `x` clipboard, Alt-mnemonics because Ctrl-b is the tmux prefix):\n\n```toml\n[metadata]\nname = \"translate\"\ndescription = \"Translation history: fuzzy-pick, preview, copy output/input, re-run\"\nrequirements = [\"translate\"]\n\n[source]\ncommand = \"translate list --tsv\"\ndisplay = \"{split:\\\\t:2}  {split:\\\\t:4}  →  {split:\\\\t:5}  ({split:\\\\t:3}) {split:\\\\t:6}\"\noutput  = \"{split:\\\\t:0}\"\nno_sort = true\n\n[preview]\ncommand = \"translate show '{split:\\\\t:0}' | bat --color=always --plain --paging=never --language=markdown\"\nheader  = \"{split:\\\\t:2}  ·  {split:\\\\t:3}\"\n\n[ui]\ninput_header = \"Translations\"\n[ui.preview_panel]\nsize = 55\n\n[keybindings]\nenter  = \"actions:copy-output\"\nctrl-y = \"actions:copy-output\"   # override tv default (whole-row) with output-only\nalt-i  = \"actions:copy-input\"\nalt-r  = \"actions:rerun\"\nalt-f  = [\"actions:favorite\", \"reload_source\"]\nalt-j  = \"actions:json\"\n\n[actions.copy-output]\ndescription = \"Copy the translation to the clipboard\"\ncommand = \"translate show '{split:\\\\t:0}' --field output | x copy\"\nmode = \"fork\"\n\n[actions.rerun]\ndescription = \"Re-translate this input in the same pair\"\ncommand = \"translate --from \\\"$(translate show '{split:\\\\t:0}' --field source_lang)\\\" --to \\\"$(translate show '{split:\\\\t:0}' --field target_lang)\\\" \\\"$(translate show '{split:\\\\t:0}' --field input)\\\" | bat --color=always --plain -l markdown | less -R; echo; read -r _\"\nmode = \"execute\"\n\n[actions.favorite]\ndescription = \"Toggle favorite\"\ncommand = \"translate history fav '{split:\\\\t:0}' --toggle\"\nmode = \"fork\"\n\n[actions.copy-input]\ndescription = \"Copy the original source text\"\ncommand = \"translate show '{split:\\\\t:0}' --field input | x copy\"\nmode = \"fork\"\n\n[actions.json]\ndescription = \"Dump record as JSON, paged\"\ncommand = \"translate show '{split:\\\\t:0}' --json | bat --color=always --plain -l json | less -R; echo; read -r _\"\nmode = \"execute\"\n```\n\n## 7. cobra command tree (`cmd/`)\n\n```\ntranslate                       root; RunE: args->once, piped stdin->once, TTY->TUI\n  translate <text...>           convenience one-shot (root.RunE, no subcommand)\n  once <text...>                explicit one-shot; reads stdin; alias: tr\n  init                          huh wizard, probes providers, writes config (§9)\n  history                       recent; --limit --pair en>es --engine X --favorite --json --tsv\n    history show <id>           render one record (also top-level `show`)\n    history fav <id>            --toggle|--on|--off\n  list                          flat tv rows; implies --tsv\n  search <q>                    FTS (sqlite) / fuzzy (jsonl); --limit --json --tsv\n  config  show|path|edit|get <key>\n  providers  list|probe         probe = live chain health (copilot/ollama/openrouter/google/dict)\n  lang  list|resolve <q>        fuzzy resolver; e.g. `lang resolve chinees` -> zh\n  completion [bash|zsh|fish|powershell]\n```\n\nPersistent flags: `--config --engine --provider --model --tier --to/-t --from/-f --json --color --no-history`; one-shot adds `--stream/--no-stream --alts N`.\n\nTypo tolerance: `rootCmd.SuggestionsMinimumDistance = 2` (cobra \"did you mean\" for subcommands) + per-cmd `SuggestFor`.\n\n**Bare-text vs subcommand disambiguation** (the one real ambiguity): cobra dispatches to a child only when `args[0]` exactly equals a child `Use`/alias; else runs `root.RunE`. So `translate init`→init, `translate \"hola mundo\"`→one-shot. Risk: `translate hisstory` typo becomes text. Mitigation in `root.RunE`: if no piped stdin **and** `len(args)==1` **and** the token is within Levenshtein distance 2 of a known subcommand, print `did you mean \"translate history\"? (translating literally — use \\`translate once <text>\\` to force)` then translate. Multi-word/quoted/piped input always translates without a prompt.\n\nRoot skeleton (pipe-vs-TTY via `x/term`):\n\n```go\nswitch {\ncase len(args) > 0:                          return oneShot(strings.Join(args,\" \"))\ncase !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())):   b,_:=io.ReadAll(os.Stdin); return oneShot(strings.TrimSpace(string(b)))\ndefault:                                      return tui.Run(resolved, store, stateStore)\n}\n```\n\nOne-shot gates ANSI on **stdout** TTY (`translate x | x copy` stays clean; `--json` for scripts). `PersistentPreRunE`: EnsureDirs, load/create config, open Store, build Resolved.\n\n## 8. Fuzzy language resolution (`internal/lang`) — shared CLI + TUI\n\n```go\ntype Lang  struct { Code, Name string; Aliases []string }   // ISO-639-1 + names/native/common typos\ntype Match struct { Code, Name string; Score float64; Exact bool }\nfunc Resolve(q string) (Match, []Match)   // best + ranked candidates (TUI disambiguation)\n```\n\nOrder: `\"auto\"` passthrough → exact code → exact name/alias/native → fuzzy via `agnivade/levenshtein` (reuses dict-fuzzy dep). `\"chinees\"→zh`, `\"spanich\"→es`, `\"中文\"→zh`. CLI resolves silently, prints `(interpreted \"chinees\" as zh)` on stderr when non-exact; TUI pops candidate `list` when ambiguous. Unknown source at translate time falls back to `whatlanggo.Detect` (offline) when the engine returns no `DetectedSource`.\n\n## 9. `translate init` wizard (huh, standalone `form.Run()`)\n\nProbe providers **before** building the form (concurrent, 2s timeout) so results show as option `Description()`s; run huh standalone (blocking), then write config. Re-run merges into existing config (`config.toml.bak` backup).\n\nProbes: copilot `GET :4141/v1/models` (strip `[1m]`, keep hyphenated, drop dated; recommend sonnet-5 / fast haiku-4-5 / max opus-4-8); ollama `GET :11434/api/tags` (expect llama3.2:3b); openrouter = `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` present; google/dict assumed reachable.\n\nForm groups:\n1. **Engine** — `Select`: Auto (recommended) / LLM / Google / Dictionary.\n2. **Provider + Model** (hidden unless engine auto|llm) — provider `Select` (options from probes, description = up/down + model count), model `Select` (from that provider's `/v1/models`, recommended one marked).\n3. **Languages** — target `Input` (fuzzy-normalized via `lang.Resolve` on write), source `Select` (Auto-detect / Fixed…).\n4. **Behavior** — live `Confirm`, debounce_ms `Input` (400), alternatives `Input` (3).\n5. **History** (hidden unless kept) — enable `Confirm`, backend `Select` (JSONL / SQLite+FTS).\n\nOn completion: start from `Default()`, overlay wizard values, fold detected-and-up providers into `[[provider]]` + `chain.order` (up first), `lang.Resolve(target)`, EnsureDirs, `config.Save`, seed `state.json`, print resolved chain + path. Non-TTY `init` errors unless `--yes --engine=… --to=…` (headless, mirrors `dotcfg --set`).\n\n## 10. Engine seam (referenced by config + history)\n\n```go\ntype TranslateResult struct {                 // Marvin-lite\n    Translation, DetectedSource, Target string\n    Alternatives []string; Notes string; Confidence float64\n}\ntype Request struct {\n    Text, Source, Target string; AltN int\n    Stream func(token string)                 // nil = non-streaming (google/dict); set = live LLM tokens\n}\ntype Engine interface {\n    Name() string\n    Available(ctx) bool                       // chain + `providers probe`\n    Translate(ctx, Request) (TranslateResult, error)\n}\nfunc BuildChain(cfg *config.Config, res config.Resolved) (Engine, error)  // walks chain.order, skips unavailable, first success; ctx ties to TUI seq/cancel\n```\n\nLLM structured output: built-in system prompt + OpenAI `response_format` `json_schema` (strict, mirrors `TranslateResult`); providers that ignore it (older Ollama) fall back to \"JSON only\" prompt + tolerant unmarshal of the first `{…}` block; total failure → raw text as `Translation`. Prompts: `Translate` (faithful/concise/register-aware) and `Interpret` (typo-tolerant, corrects silently, records the correction in `Notes`). Grammar-correction/vocab/spaced-review are future.\n\n## 11. First-run + open questions\n\n- Any first command: EnsureDirs + write `Default()` if missing + hint to run `init`.\n- Validate `google`/`dict` reserved chain ids don't collide with a `[[provider]].name` (error on clash).\n- Implementer choices: (a) ULID over UUID (time-sortable → no ts sort in sqlite `Recent`); (b) empty piped input = usage error, not TUI; (c) MyMemory's 500-byte/q + 5k/day caps make it a fallback only — length-guard before falling through."
```
