CC-CEDICT (Chinese → English)\n\n- **Download URL (canonical):** `https://www.mdbg.net/chinese/export/cedict/cedict_1_0_ts_utf-8_mdbg.txt.gz`\n- **Sizes (measured):** gz **3,966,510 B (~3.8 MB)** → uncompressed **9,829,714 B (~9.4 MB)**.\n- **Counts (measured):** 124,781 total lines = 30 comment lines (`#…`) + **124,751 entries**.\n- **Line format:** `Traditional Simplified [pin1 yin1] /sense1/sense2/…/`\n  - Trad and Simp separated by a single space.\n  - Pinyin in `[ ]`, syllables space-separated with a trailing tone digit `1–5` (5 = neutral); `ü` written `u:`; proper nouns capitalized.\n  - Definitions wrapped in slashes; **senses separated by `/`**, glosses within one sense separated by `;`.\n  - Real example line: `皮實 皮实 [pi2 shi5] /(of things) durable/(of people) sturdy; tough/`\n- **Go parse regex:** `^(\\S+)\\s+(\\S+)\\s+\\[([^\\]]*)\\]\\s+/(.*)/\\s*$` → groups = trad, simp, pinyin, defs; then `strings.Split(defsGroup, \"/\")` and drop empties. Skip lines starting with `#`.\n- **Building BOTH indexes:** parse each line into one `*Entry{Trad, Simp, Pinyin, Defs}`. Insert into two maps keyed by `Trad` and by `Simp`, both pointing at the same `*Entry`. Use `map[string][]*Entry` (slice values): multiple traditional forms collapse to one simplified key (heteronyms/variants), so collisions are real. When `Trad == Simp` (very common), insert once.\n- **RAM if kept in memory:** ~125k entries / 9.4 MB text → roughly **40–80 MB** resident for the two maps. Acceptable to hold in RAM (unlike ECDICT below).\n- **License (confirmed in the file header, not just secondhand):** **CC BY-SA 4.0** (`https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/`), referenced work `CEDICT Copyright (C) 1997, 1998 Paul Andrew Denisowski`. Obligation = **attribution + share-alike**; ship the header/attribution text with the app.\n- **\"Don't scrape\" caveat:** use the downloadable export file only; do **not** scrape mdbg.net's online query pages. The dict-format wiki also warns that CC-CEDICT and others copy from each other, so bogus entries propagate — don't treat every gloss as authoritative.\n\n## 2. ECDICT (English → Chinese) — `github.com/skywind3000/ECDICT`, **MIT license**\n\n- **`ecdict.csv` (master branch, \"basic 76万\" edition) — measured:** **65,933,428 B (~62.9 MB)** uncompressed, **770,612 lines → ~770,611 entries**. Raw URL: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/skywind3000/ECDICT/master/ecdict.csv`\n- **`ecdict.mini.csv`: only 4,204 B** — a tiny sample/template, NOT a usable subset. Ignore it.\n- **`stardict.7z` (in master; a SQLite `stardict.db` of the basic 770k edition): 51,577,449 B (~49.2 MB)** compressed. Downside: `.7z` has no Go stdlib decompressor (would need `github.com/bodgit/sevenzip`).\n- **Release 1.0.28 assets (the FULL 340万 / 3.4M-entry edition — overkill):**\n  - `ecdict-sqlite-28.zip`: **216,765,132 B (~206.7 MB zip)** → uncompressed ~1 GB+.\n  - `ecdict-stardict-28.zip`: **70,415,834 B (~67.2 MB)**.\n- **CSV schema (13 columns, header row present, RFC-4180 quoted):**\n  `word, phonetic, definition, translation, pos, collins, oxford, tag, bnc, frq, exchange, detail, audio`\n  - `word` = English headword; `phonetic` = IPA (no slashes).\n  - `definition` = English glosses; senses joined by literal `\\n` (backslash-n, not a real newline).\n  - **`translation` = Chinese glosses** (the E→C payload); senses joined by literal `\\n`.\n  - `pos` = POS ratios; `collins` = 0–5 star; `oxford` = 1 if Oxford-3000; `tag` = level tags (`zk gk cet4 cet6 ky toefl ielts gre`); `bnc`/`frq` = frequency ranks.\n  - `exchange` = morphology, format `type:form/type:form` (`0`=lemma, `p`=past, `d`=past-participle, `i`=-ing, `3`=3rd-sg, `r`=comparative, `t`=superlative, `s`=plural). Useful to normalize an inflected query back to its lemma before lookup.\n  - `detail`/`audio` usually empty. Use `encoding/csv` (fields contain commas inside quotes); the `\\n` inside translation/definition is a literal escape, not an embedded record break.\n- **CSV-in-RAM cost (~770k rows):** keeping just `word`+`translation`+`phonetic` ≈ **150–250 MB** resident (Go string + map overhead); all 13 columns ≈ 300 MB+. Too heavy for a mostly-idle TUI.\n- **Recommendation → SQLite via `modernc.org/sqlite` (pure Go, already a viable dep), but NOT the 207 MB full release.** Lazy-download `ecdict.csv` (63 MB) once, then **build a local SQLite on first run**: table with `word`, `word_lc` (lowercased, indexed), `translation`, `phonetic`, `exchange`. Resulting `.db` ~80–100 MB on disk; you can delete the CSV afterward. Queries are on-disk, **~0 steady-state RAM**:\n  - exact: `WHERE word_lc = ?`\n  - prefix suggestions: `WHERE word_lc LIKE ?||'%' ORDER BY frq LIMIT n`\n  - fuzzy: run the existing levenshtein only over a prefix-narrowed candidate set, never a 770k scan.\n  This avoids the RAM blowup, the `.7z` dependency, and the 3.4M full dataset. (Building your own DB from the MIT CSV is cleaner than shipping their `stardict.db`.)\n\n## 3. moedict (`moedict.tw`) — zh-zh monolingual\n\n- **Base host:** `https://www.moedict.tw` (query this; `moedict.org` returns CORS errors). Endpoints:\n  - `/raw/&lt;word&gt;` — original JSON, non-Big5 chars as `{[abcd]}` placeholders.\n  - `/uni/&lt;word&gt;` — same JSON but real Unicode glyphs → **use this** programmatically.\n  - `/pua/&lt;word&gt;` — PUA codepoints (needs the MOEDICT font).\n  - `/a/&lt;word&gt;.json` Mandarin (tokenized), `/t/…` Taiwanese Hokkien, `/h/…` Hakka, `/c/…` cross-strait.\n  - JSONP: append `?callback=moedict_jsonp_callback`.\n- **JSON shape (`/uni/`):** `title`, `radical`, `stroke_count`, `non_radical_stroke_count`, `heteronyms[]` → each has `bopomofo`, `bopomofo2`, `pinyin`, `definitions[]` → each has `def`, `type`, optional `example[]`, `quote[]`, `link[]`; optional top-level `translation{}` with per-language arrays.\n- **License:** MOE source texts are **CC BY-ND 3.0 Taiwan** (no-derivatives; MOE retains copyright); the g0v repo/contribution is CC0; embedded CC-CEDICT/CFDict/HanDeDict tables are CC BY-SA 4.0.\n- **Verdict — DEFER past v1.** It is zh→zh (definitions in Chinese, not English, so it doesn't serve the C↔E core), **online-only** (breaks the offline promise), and **CC BY-ND** makes caching/redistributing derivatives legally awkward. Keep it only as an optional *online* zh-zh fallback for words CC-CEDICT misses; not worth wiring for v1.\n\n## 4. Download-on-first-use plan\n\n- **Location:** under `internal/xdgpath.DataDir()` → `~/.local/share/translate/dicts/`. Files: `cedict.txt.gz` (or expanded `cedict.txt`), `ecdict.db` (built locally), plus a small `manifest.json` (source URLs, version, sizes, checksums).\n- **One-time download total:** CC-CEDICT **~3.8 MB gz** + ECDICT **~63 MB CSV** = **~67 MB**. After building `ecdict.db` you may delete the CSV; steady-state on-disk footprint ~90–110 MB.\n- **Strategy:** lazy-download on **first dict use**, not at startup. Show a one-line TUI footer progress note (\"Downloading dictionaries… 42/67 MB\", then \"Building index…\"). CC-CEDICT: gunzip + parse into in-RAM trad/simp maps (fast, ~40–80 MB). ECDICT: build SQLite once (~seconds), then query on disk via `modernc.org/sqlite`.\n- **Detection / routing (plugs into `internal/engine/dict.go`):** if the input contains Han characters (`unicode.Han`) → CC-CEDICT (zh→en, in-memory maps); else → ECDICT (en→zh, SQLite). Exact match first; on miss, emit prefix/fuzzy candidates through the **existing ranked-suggestion list** (`TranslateResult.Suggestions`) — do not auto-pick.\n\n## v1 data plan (concrete)\n\n- **Sources:** CC-CEDICT (zh→en) + ECDICT basic 770k (en→zh). **Drop dictionaryapi.dev.** Defer moedict.\n- **Storage split (asymmetric, by size):** CC-CEDICT → in-memory `map[string][]*Entry` on both trad &amp; simp keys. ECDICT → **locally-built SQLite** from `ecdi

# Unified implementation plan — presets + cache, local bilingual dict

## Reconciled decisions (conflicts resolved; web-verified facts win)

1. **ECDICT delivery — build SQLite locally from the CSV, do NOT download a prebuilt DB.** Input (3) proposed downloading `ecdict-sqlite-28.zip` ("~30–50 MB zip / ~300 MB db"). Web-verified sizes (input 1) show that asset is the **3.4M-entry full edition: 206.7 MB zip → ~1 GB uncompressed** (overkill), and the only prebuilt *basic* DB ships as `.7z` (no Go stdlib decoder). **Decision:** download `ecdict.csv` (62.9 MB, MIT) from `raw.githubusercontent.com/skywind3000/ECDICT/master/ecdict.csv`, build a local SQLite via `modernc.org/sqlite` (pure-Go, no CGo) → **~80–100 MB `.db`**, delete the CSV. Avoids the `.7z` dependency, the 1 GB blob, and RAM blowup; we own the schema.
2. **dictionaryapi.dev — replace as default, keep as opt-in.** Input (1) said "drop it"; input (3) said "keep selectable." **Decision:** local bilingual dict is the default (`dict.source = "local"`). The existing `DictEngine` stays compiled and reachable via `source = "api"`, plus an optional English-miss `api_fallback` (default **off**). Non-destructive, offline-first, and still gives English synonyms/examples ECDICT lacks.
3. **Download strategy — explicit command primary.** **Decision:** `translate dict update [cedict|ecdict|all]` is the one blessed path for both sources. Only CC-CEDICT (~4 MB) may auto-download inline on first Chinese lookup when `auto_download=true` (default **false**). ECDICT (63 MB + build) **never** blocks a keystroke; absent data degrades to a friendly `Notes` prompt.
4. **Force-refresh gesture.** Terminals send CR for both Enter and Ctrl+Enter; do not depend on the distinction. **Decision:** Enter = the force-fresh gesture (today's `launch(true)`, zero key-handling change); the cache "live feel" comes entirely from the debounce path (`launch(false)`). Add `"ctrl+enter"` as a harmless alias on the `Translate` binding.
5. **Presets.** Three styles — `concise` (default, current prompt verbatim), `contextual`, `dictionary`. Switch key **`ctrl+y`** (verified free: `c/l/e/t/p/r/u`, enter, alt+enter, esc are taken). Plain-text streaming preserved; no JSON, no new result fields.
6. **moedict — deferred** (zh-zh, online-only, CC BY-ND). Not v1.

---

## PHASE 1 — LLM translate presets

Independently shippable. Engine stays UI-free; presets are pure prompt-data selection. No dependency on later phases.

**`internal/engine/prompt.go`**
- Rename `translateSystemPrompt` → `translateSystemPromptConcise` (text verbatim).
- Add `translateSystemPromptContextual`, `translateSystemPromptDictionary` (the two prompts from input (2) §A3 are good as-is — short list of senses; translation + 1–2 examples with `↳` back-translation).
- Add id constants + selector:
```go
const (
    PresetConcise    = "concise"
    PresetContextual = "contextual"
    PresetDictionary = "dictionary"
)
func systemPromptFor(preset string) string // switch; default → concise
```
- `buildTranslatePrompt` final line: `return systemPromptFor(req.Preset), fmt.Sprintf(translateUserPrompt, src, tgt, req.Text)`. User prompt unchanged. **No `llm.go` change** (both transports already call `buildTranslatePrompt(req)`).

**`internal/engine/engine.go`** — add one field to `Request`:
```go
Preset string // "" => PresetConcise (LLM translate only; google/dict ignore)
```

**`internal/config/config.go`** — `General` gains `Preset string \`toml:"preset"\``; `Default()` sets `Preset: "concise"`.

**`internal/config/resolve.go`** — `Overrides.Preset`, `Resolved.Preset`; in `Resolve()`: `Preset: pick(o.Preset, "TRANSLATE_PRESET", c.General.Preset)`.

**`cmd/root.go`** — `var flagPreset string`; `f.StringVar(&flagPreset, "preset", "", "LLM prompt style: concise|contextual|dictionary")`; set in `overrides()`; `oneShot(... preset string)` param → `Request.Preset = preset` (both call sites pass `res.Preset`); `runTUI` sets `Params.Preset: res.Preset`.

**`internal/tui/`** — `Params.Preset`, `Model.preset` (default `PresetConcise` if empty), `launch()` `engine.Request` adds `Preset: m.preset`. Picker overlay mirrors the model picker exactly:
- `keys.go`: `PickPreset key.Binding` = `ctrl+y` (help `^y`/`style`); add `"ctrl+enter"` to the `Translate` keys.
- `pickers.go`: `overlayPreset` in the iota; `presetItem{id, desc string; current bool}`; `presetItems(current)` with the 3 entries.
- `model.go`: `presetList list.Model` built in `New()` (title `"Style (↵ select · esc close)"`, `SetShowHelp(false)`), sized in `relayout()`.
- `update.go`: `handleKey` case for `PickPreset` (guard `m.active().Mode == engine.ModeDict` → no-op); `overlayListUpdate` + `overlaySelect` cases (on select set `m.preset`; if live+non-empty call `m.launch(false)` — cache-aware once Phase 2 lands, plain re-fetch before).
- `view.go`: `overlayBody` case; footer non-dict branch adds `m.st.dim.Render("style:"+m.preset)` segment and `^y style` in the help string.

**Verification (Phase 1):** `go build ./...`; `translate --preset contextual "bank" --to zh` and `--preset dictionary` show the distinct shapes; `TRANSLATE_PRESET=dictionary translate ...` honored; in TUI press `^y`, pick each style, confirm footer `style:<x>` and that output shape changes; confirm `^y` is inert in dictionary mode. Streaming still token-by-token.

---

## PHASE 2 — Result cache + force-refresh (TUI only)

Independently shippable; builds on Phase 1's preset in the key. No engine changes, no locking (single-goroutine Update loop).

**New `internal/tui/cache.go`**
```go
type cacheKey struct { preset, engine, model, source, target, text string } // comparable → map key
func (m Model) cacheKeyFor(text string) cacheKey // {m.preset, m.active().Name, m.modelOverride, m.source, m.target, text}
```
Keyed on the *selected* engine identity (e.g. `"auto"`), so a chain fallback still caches under the user-visible engine.

**`internal/tui/model.go`**
- Add `cache map[cacheKey]*engine.TranslateResult` (init in `New()`), `pendingKey cacheKey`.
- **Change** `lastDone string` → `lastDoneKey cacheKey`. This is the one real refactor: today's `text == m.lastDone` guard is preset/engine-blind and would make preset/engine switches on identical text silently no-op. (The dict-miss "second Enter" guard at update.go:182 uses `m.lastInput`, not `lastDone`, so it is unaffected.)

**`internal/tui/update.go`**
- Top of `launch(force)`: compute `key := m.cacheKeyFor(text)`; no-op guard becomes `if !force && key == m.lastDoneKey && m.status == statusDone`. Then, **only when `!force`**, a cache hit renders instantly with **no API call**: `cancelInflight`, set `status=statusDone`, `result=cached`, stamp `curEngine/curModel` from cached, `vp.SetContent(m.renderResult(*cached))`, `lastDoneKey=key`, return. On miss/force: existing fetch body, plus `m.pendingKey = key` captured before the goroutine.
- `handleStream` `ChunkDone`: `m.lastDoneKey = m.pendingKey`; and where the result is non-nil, `m.cache[m.pendingKey] = msg.chunk.Result` (write-through; `force` naturally overwrites since the fetch always ran). Never cache on `ChunkError`. History-save logic unchanged.

**Semantics:** live typing/debounce → `launch(false)` → **hit = instant, no call; miss = stream + cache**. Enter (or the `ctrl+enter` alias) → `launch(true)` → **always fresh, overwrites**. Switching preset/lang/model/engine produces a different key, so stale styles never leak. Session-scoped unbounded map (small; fine for v1). `^u` deliberately does not clear it (retype should still hit).

**Verification (Phase 2):** enable live; type a word, edit, retype the original → second render is instant with no spinner and no network (confirm via a provider log or `nc`-style stub); press Enter on the same text → spinner + fresh call overwrites; switch `^y`/`^t`/`^p` → distinct fetches, each independently cached; `esc`/quit clears (process-scoped). Add a unit test on `cacheKeyFor` equality/inequality across each field.

---

## PHASE 3 — Dictionary data layer + `dict update` (no routing change yet)

Independently shippable: adds a command and config, touches nothing in the hot path. Default stays `api` until Phase 4 flips it, so this phase is safe to land alone.

**`go.mod`** — add `modernc.org/sqlite` (pure-Go; ~6–9 MB binary growth — see tradeoffs).

**`internal/config/config.go`** — extend `Dict`:
```go
Source       string `toml:"source"`        // local | api   (default local)
Dir          string `toml:"dir,omitempty"` // "" => DataDir()/dict
CedictPath   string `toml:"cedict_path,omitempty"`
EcdictPath   string `toml:"ecdict_path,omitempty"` // built .db
CedictURL    string `toml:"cedict_url,omitempty"`
EcdictURL    string `toml:"ecdict_url,omitempty"`  // CSV, not a release zip
AutoDownload bool   `toml:"auto_download"`         // CC-CEDICT only
APIFallback  bool   `toml:"api_fallback"`
```
`Default()`: `Source:"local"`, `AutoDownload:false`, `APIFallback:false`, keep existing `Endpoint`/`Lang` (for `source=api`), and the **verified** URLs:
- `CedictURL = "https://www.mdbg.net/chinese/export/cedict/cedict_1_0_ts_utf-8_mdbg.txt.gz"`
- `EcdictURL = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/skywind3000/ECDICT/master/ecdict.csv"`

**New `internal/engine/dictdata.go`** — shared paths + download/build:
```go
func DictDir(cfg LocalDictConfig) string
func downloadCedict(ctx, url, dst string) error            // GET .gz → gzip → write cedict_ts.u8 (atomic tmp+rename)
func buildEcdictDB(ctx, csvURL, dbPath string, prog func(string)) error // GET csv → encoding/csv → sqlite
```
Local ECDICT schema we build (own it; drop stardict/.7z):
```sql
CREATE TABLE entries(
  word TEXT, word_lc TEXT, phonetic TEXT, translation TEXT,
  definition TEXT, pos TEXT, frq INTEGER, exchange TEXT);
CREATE INDEX idx_word_lc ON entries(word_lc);
```
Parse the 13-col RFC-4180 CSV with `encoding/csv` (commas inside quotes; the `\n` in `translation`/`definition` is a literal backslash-n). Build inside one transaction (~seconds). Verify with `SELECT count(*)`. All downloads write `*.tmp` then `os.Rename` (mirrors `config.Save`).

**New `cmd/dictupdate.go`** — `translate dict update [cedict|ecdict|all]`: `xdgpath.EnsureDirs()`, `os.MkdirAll(Dir,0700)`, progress via `dustin/go-humanize` (already indirect dep). This is a sibling of `define`; register in `NewRootCmd`.

**`internal/lang/detect.go`** — add (dependency-free Han scan):
```go
func IsChinese(text string) bool // any unicode.Is(unicode.Han, r) → true
```

**Verification (Phase 3):** `translate dict update cedict` writes `~/.local/share/translate/dict/cedict_ts.u8` (~9.4 MB) and prints progress; `translate dict update ecdict` produces `ecdict.db` (~80–100 MB), `sqlite3 ... "select count(*) from entries"` ≈ 770k; re-run is atomic/idempotent; `IsChinese("你好")==true`, `IsChinese("hello")==false`, `IsChinese("wifi密码")==true`. Unit-test the CC-CEDICT line parser and a `:memory:` ECDICT build on a 3-row CSV fixture.

---

## PHASE 4 — LocalDictEngine + route by script + flip default

Depends on Phase 3. Flips the dictionary to bilingual local; `Name()=="dictionary"` throughout so `^e` cycle, `NamedEngine{Mode:ModeDict}`, history, and `cmd/define.go` are untouched.

**New `internal/engine/localdict.go`, `cedict.go`, `ecdict.go`**
```go
type LocalDictConfig struct {
    Dir, CedictPath, EcdictPath, CedictURL, EcdictURL string
    AutoDownload, Fuzzy, APIFallbackOn bool
    APIFallback *DictEngine
    Timeout time.Duration
}
type LocalDictEngine struct { cfg LocalDictConfig; ce *cedictIndex; ec *ecdictDB } // both lazy sync.Once
func NewLocalDict(cfg LocalDictConfig) *LocalDictEngine
func (e *LocalDictEngine) Name() string { return "dictionary" }
func (e *LocalDictEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeDict }
func (e *LocalDictEngine) Detect(context.Context, string) (string, error) { return "", nil }
func (e *LocalDictEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool // ≥1 data file present
func (e *LocalDictEngine) Translate(ctx, req) (<-chan Chunk, error) // route: lang.IsChinese → lookupZh else lookupEn
```
- `cedictIndex`: `byKey map[string][]*cedictEntry` on both simp & trad, sorted `keys []string` for prefix suggestions; parse `^(\S+)\s+(\S+)\s+\[([^\]]*)\]\s+/(.*)/\s*$`, split defs on `/`, skip `#`. Miss → **prefix-only** suggestions (`sort.SearchStrings` + `HasPrefix`, capped `suggestLimit`); no levenshtein over Han.
- `ecdictDB`: `sql.Open("sqlite", path)`, `PRAGMA query_only`. Exact: `WHERE word_lc = ? LIMIT 1`. Miss suggestions: `WHERE word_lc LIKE ?||'%' ORDER BY frq DESC LIMIT 64`, then rerank with the existing `agnivade/levenshtein`, cut to `suggestLimit`. Falls back to the current `/usr/share/dict/words` `wordIndex` only if the `.db` is absent.
- **Map into the unchanged `DictEntry`/`Suggestions` shape** so `renderDictionary`/`renderSuggestions`/`define.go` need no changes: CC-CEDICT → `Word:Simp` (or `简/繁`), `Phonetic:pinyin`, one `Meaning` per pinyin reading (`PartOfSpeech` = the reading), glosses → `Definitions[].Text`. ECDICT → `Word`, `Phonetic`, Chinese `translation` lines → `Definitions[].Text`; optionally a second `Meaning{PartOfSpeech:"en"}` with the English `definition`.
- Missing data → `single(&TranslateResult{Engine:"dictionary", Notes:"dictionary not installed — run `translate dict update`"}, nil)`; CC-CEDICT + `auto_download` → inline `.gz` fetch honoring `ctx`, emitting a `ChunkToken` status first. Respect `ctx.Err()` so live-typing cancellation isn't surfaced as an error.
- Optional `APIFallback` (English miss, `api_fallback=true`) → call the API engine before returning suggestions; Chinese never falls back.

**`cmd/build.go`** — `dictFromConfig` becomes a factory returning the interface:
```go
func dictFromConfig(cfg *config.Config) engine.Engine {
    if cfg.Dict.Source == "api" { return engine.NewDict(...) } // today's behavior
    return engine.NewLocalDict(engine.LocalDictConfig{ /* Dir, URLs, AutoDownload, Fuzzy, APIFallback... */ })
}
```
`buildEngineSet` and `cmd/define.go` are unchanged (both already treat it via the `Engine` interface / `NamedEngine`).

**`internal/tui/`** — bilingual footer (view.go:98–107): label `"dictionary (zh↔en)"`, or `"dictionary ("+m.curDictDir+")"` after a lookup. Add `curDictDir string` to `Model`, stamped in `handleStream` from the result (route direction). Keep `↵ look up  ^l live  ^e engine  ^r history  ^c quit` (no `^t`/`^p`/`^y` in dict mode — both languages fixed by source script). Suggest-overlay footer already reads correctly.

**Verification (Phase 4):** with data installed — `translate define 你好` (zh→en glosses + pinyin), `translate define ephemeral` (en→zh translation + IPA), `translate define teh` → ranked English suggestions (no auto-pick), `translate define 世` → prefix suggestions; `--json` shows `Dictionary`/`Suggestions`. In TUI: `^e` to dictionary, type Chinese then English, confirm footer direction flips and suggestion overlay works. Without data: friendly `Notes`, no crash, no keystroke hang. Flip `source=api` in config → old dictionaryapi.dev path still works. Table-test routing + miss→Suggestions with tiny `testdata/` CE file and `:memory:` DB.

---

## Deferred (explicit, with reasons)

- **moedict (zh-zh)** — online-only (breaks offline promise), definitions in Chinese not English (doesn't serve C↔E core), CC BY-ND makes caching derivatives legally awkward. Keep only as a future optional *online* zh-zh fallback.
- **ECDICT full 3.4M edition + prebuilt `stardict.db`/`.7z`** — 1 GB uncompressed / needs a non-stdlib 7z decoder. The locally-built 770k SQLite covers v1.
- **Persisting the result cache to disk** — LLM output is nondeterministic, so persisted entries feel "stuck" across sessions. If ever added: TTL + LRU cap. v1 stays session-only.
- **Engine-layer cache decorator shared with the one-shot CLI** — CLI is one-shot, gains little; keeping the cache in the TUI keeps the engine layer UI-free.
- **Ctrl+Enter as a semantically distinct gesture** — unreliable across terminals; shipped only as a harmless alias of Enter.
- **Pinyin tone-number → tone-mark prettifying, POS-ratio prettifying, morphology lemma-normalization before ECDICT lookup** — nice-to-have polish; the `exchange` column is stored so lemma-normalization can be added later without a re-download.
- **CC-CEDICT CC BY-SA attribution UX** — must ship the header/attribution text with the app; wire the exact placement (about/`--license`) as a small follow-up, not a blocker.

---

## Tradeoffs & mitigations

- **Binary size:** `modernc.org/sqlite` adds ~6–9 MB (pure-Go, cross-compiles cleanly — worth it vs. CGo or a hand-rolled on-disk index). Mitigation: it's the only new dep; CC-CEDICT and the cache need none.
- **Disk:** ECDICT `.db` ~80–100 MB, CC-CEDICT ~9.4 MB, one-time downloads ~67 MB. Mitigation: explicit `dict update`, delete the CSV post-build, ECDICT strictly opt-in.
- **RAM:** CC-CEDICT in-memory maps ~40–80 MB, **lazy** (only after first Chinese lookup). ECDICT stays on disk — indexed point lookups, ~0 steady-state RAM. Cache map is tiny (session-scoped pointers).
- **Latency/startup:** zero added to app start (all dict data lazy). First Chinese lookup pays a one-time ~50–150 ms parse; first English lookup pays `sql.Open` + query (single-digit ms). After that, dict lookups are instant/offline — and the Feature 2 cache makes repeat *LLM* queries instant too.
- **Cold/offline UX:** missing data degrades to a clear `Notes` prompt, never an error or a keystroke-time 63 MB fetch; only the 4 MB CC-CEDICT may auto-install when explicitly enabled.
- **Fuzzy quality:** English "did you mean" is strong (prefix + `frq` + levenshtein over real headwords); Chinese is prefix-only by design (CJK edit distance is noisy) — matches the existing "don't auto-pick, show ranked list" contract.

Relevant files (all absolute): `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/engine/{prompt.go,engine.go,llm.go,dict.go}`, `.../internal/config/{config.go,resolve.go}`, `.../cmd/{root.go,build.go,define.go}`, `.../internal/tui/{model.go,update.go,view.go,keys.go,pickers.go}`, `.../internal/lang/detect.go`, `.../internal/xdgpath/paths.go`; new: `.../internal/tui/cache.go`, `.../internal/engine/{localdict.go,cedict.go,ecdict.go,dictdata.go}`, `.../cmd/dictupdate.go`.

I've read all the relevant code. No `preset` concept exists yet. Here is the concrete implementation plan for Feature 1, written against the actual code.

---

# Feature 1 — LLM Presets + Result Cache: Design

## Key facts that shape the design
- `buildTranslatePrompt(req Request) (system, user string)` in `prompt.go` is the *single* prompt builder; both `translateOpenAI` and `translateAnthropic` call it. So preset selection needs to touch exactly one function.
- `finalize()` puts the whole streamed text into `TranslateResult.Translation` as trimmed plain text. Presets (b)/(c) emit multi-line plain text — this flows through unchanged: the TUI streams `m.streamBuf` and finally renders `res.Translation` via `renderResult`. **No JSON, no new result fields, streaming preserved.**
- Ctrl keys already in use: `c l e t p r u`, plus `enter`, `alt+enter`, `esc`. `ctrl+m`==Enter, `ctrl+i`==Tab, `ctrl+h`==Backspace, `ctrl+j`==LF, `ctrl+s`/`ctrl+q`==flow-control — all unavailable. **`ctrl+y` is free and safe** (recommended for the preset picker); `ctrl+o`/`ctrl+n` are fallbacks.
- The existing `launch(force)` short-circuit `text == m.lastDone` is keyed only on text — it must become preset/engine-aware (see cache section) or preset switches silently no-op.

---

## PART A — PRESETS

### A1. Preset identifiers (engine package, `prompt.go`)
Add exported constants so config/TUI reference the same strings:

```go
// Preset selects the LLM translate prompt style. Only ModeTranslate LLM engines
// honor it; google/dict ignore it.
const (
    PresetConcise    = "concise"    // terse direct translation (default)
    PresetContextual = "contextual" // short list of common-sense translations
    PresetDictionary = "dictionary" // translation + 1-2 example sentences
)
```

### A2. Request carries the preset (`engine/engine.go`)
Add one field to `Request` (no other struct changes):

```go
type Request struct {
    Text          string
    Source        string
    Target        string
    Mode          Mode
    MaxAlts       int
    Stream        bool
    Model         string
    ModelProvider string
    Preset        string // "" => PresetConcise (LLM translate only)
}
```

### A3. Three system prompts + selector (`prompt.go`)
Rename the current const to `translateSystemPromptConcise` (keep its exact text). Add two more, plus a selector. `buildTranslatePrompt` changes one line; the **user prompt stays identical** for all presets.

```go
const translateSystemPromptConcise = /* current translateSystemPrompt text, verbatim */

const translateSystemPromptContextual = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.
Translate the user's text into the target language, showing how the translation changes
across the most common senses or contexts of the source.

Rules:
- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. The text may contain typos, slang, or ungrammatical
  phrasing — interpret the INTENDED meaning; never refuse and never translate a typo literally.
- Output a SHORT list of 2-4 lines, one line per distinct common sense/context.
- Format each line EXACTLY as: "N. <translation> — <short context label in the TARGET language>".
- Order lines from the most likely/common sense to the least.
- If the text has only one natural sense, output a single line — do not invent senses.
- Output ONLY the list. No preamble, no headings, no commentary before or after.`

const translateSystemPromptDictionary = `You are a precise, professional translation engine
that also gives brief usage examples.
Translate the user's text into the target language, then illustrate it with example sentences.

Rules:
- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. Interpret the INTENDED meaning of typos/slang; never refuse.
- The FIRST line is ONLY the translation of the user's text — no label, no quotes.
- Then a blank line, then 1-2 example sentences that use the translation naturally.
- Format each example on two lines:
    line 1: the example sentence in the TARGET language,
    line 2: its translation in the source language (the language of the user's input),
            prefixed with "  ↳ ".
- Keep examples short and idiomatic. Output ONLY the translation and the examples —
  no headings, no numbering of the first line, no commentary.`

func systemPromptFor(preset string) string {
    switch preset {
    case PresetContextual:
        return translateSystemPromptContextual
    case PresetDictionary:
        return translateSystemPromptDictionary
    default: // PresetConcise or unknown/empty
        return translateSystemPromptConcise
    }
}
```

`buildTranslatePrompt` last line becomes:
```go
return systemPromptFor(req.Preset), fmt.Sprintf(translateUserPrompt, src, tgt, req.Text)
```
No changes needed in `llm.go` — both transports already funnel through `buildTranslatePrompt(req)`.

### A4. Config default (`config/config.go`)
Add to `General`:
```go
Preset string `toml:"preset"` // concise | contextual | dictionary
```
In `Default()` set `Preset: "concise"`. Unknown values are harmless (`systemPromptFor` defaults to concise).

### A5. Resolve + CLI flag (`config/resolve.go`, `cmd/root.go`)
- `Overrides`: add `Preset string`.
- `Resolved`: add `Preset string`.
- In `Resolve()`: `Preset: pick(o.Preset, "TRANSLATE_PRESET", c.General.Preset)`.
- `cmd/root.go`: add `var flagPreset string`; register `f.StringVar(&flagPreset, "preset", "", "LLM prompt style: concise|contextual|dictionary")`; set `Preset: flagPreset` in `overrides()`.
- `oneShot`: add a `preset string` param and set `Preset: preset` in its `engine.Request`; both call sites pass `res.Preset`.

### A6. TUI wiring (`tui/model.go`)
- `Params`: add `Preset string`.
- `Model`: add `preset string`.
- `New()`: `preset: p.Preset` (fall back to `engine.PresetConcise` if empty).
- `launch()` `engine.Request` literal: add `Preset: m.preset`.
- `runTUI` (`cmd/root.go`): `Params{… Preset: res.Preset}`.

### A7. Preset picker overlay
Mirror the existing model picker exactly (it's the closest analog — a static list, no async fetch).

1	package engine

3	import (

8	)

10	// translateSystemPrompt drives the primary (hot-path) translation. It asks for
11	// ONLY the translated text — no JSON, no commentary — so the reply streams
12	// token-by-token identically across every OpenAI-compatible backend and is
13	// latency-optimal. Richer output (alternatives/notes/confidence) is a separate,
14	// optional enrichment pass, not part of this stream.
15	const translateSystemPrompt = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.
16	Translate the user's text into the target language faithfully and idiomatically.
17	
18	Rules:
19	- Preserve the meaning, tone, and register (formal/informal, technical/casual) of the source.
20	- The user's text may contain typos, slang, or ungrammatical phrasing. Interpret their
21	  INTENDED meaning and translate that; do not translate a typo literally, and never refuse.
22	- Output ONLY the translated text. No quotes, no explanations, no language labels,
23	  no commentary before or after.
24	- If the source language equals the target language, return the text unchanged (lightly
25	  corrected if it was misspelled).`
26	
27	// translateUserPrompt frames the concrete request.
28	const translateUserPrompt = `Source language: %s
29	Target language: %s
30	
31	Text:
32	%s`
33	
34	// buildTranslatePrompt returns the (system, user) messages for a plain-text
35	// translation request. Language codes are expanded to readable names so the
36	// model handles regional variants (e.g. zh-TW → "chinese (traditional)").
37	func buildTranslatePrompt(req Request) (system, user string) {
38		src := strings.TrimSpace(req.Source)
39		if src == "" || src == "auto" {
40			src = "auto"
41		} else {
42			src = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(src), src)
43		}
44		tgt := fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(req.Target), req.Target)
45		return translateSystemPrompt, fmt.Sprintf(translateUserPrompt, src, tgt, req.Text)
46	}

<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package engine

import (
	"fmt"
	"strings"

	"translate/internal/lang"
)

// Preset selects the LLM translate prompt style. Only ModeTranslate LLM engines
// honor it; google/dict ignore it.
const (
	PresetConcise    = "concise"    // terse direct translation (default)
	PresetContextual = "contextual" // short list of common-sense translations
	PresetDictionary = "dictionary" // translation + 1-2 example sentences
)

// translateSystemPromptConcise drives the primary (hot-path) translation. It asks
// for ONLY the translated text so the reply streams token-by-token identically
// across every backend and is latency-optimal.
const translateSystemPromptConcise = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.
Translate the user's text into the target language faithfully and idiomatically.

Rules:
- Preserve the meaning, tone, and register (formal/informal, technical/casual) of the source.
- The user's text may contain typos, slang, or ungrammatical phrasing. Interpret their
  INTENDED meaning and translate that; do not translate a typo literally, and never refuse.
- Output ONLY the translated text. No quotes, no explanations, no language labels,
  no commentary before or after.
- If the source language equals the target language, return the text unchanged (lightly
  corrected if it was misspelled).`

// translateSystemPromptContextual lists translations across common senses.
const translateSystemPromptContextual = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.
Translate the user's text into the target language, showing how the translation changes
across the most common senses or contexts of the source.

Rules:
- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. The text may contain typos, slang, or ungrammatical
  phrasing — interpret the INTENDED meaning; never refuse and never translate a typo literally.
- Output a SHORT list of 2-4 lines, one line per distinct common sense/context.
- Format each line EXACTLY as: "N. <translation> — <short context label in the TARGET language>".
- Order lines from the most likely/common sense to the least.
- If the text has only one natural sense, output a single line — do not invent senses.
- Output ONLY the list. No preamble, no headings, no commentary before or after.`

// translateSystemPromptDictionary gives a translation plus example sentences.
const translateSystemPromptDictionary = `You are a precise, professional translation engine
that also gives brief usage examples.
Translate the user's text into the target language, then illustrate it with example sentences.

Rules:
- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. Interpret the INTENDED meaning of typos/slang; never refuse.
- The FIRST line is ONLY the translation of the user's text — no label, no quotes.
- Then a blank line, then 1-2 example sentences that use the translation naturally.
- Format each example on two lines:
    line 1: the example sentence in the TARGET language,
    line 2: its translation in the source language (the language of the user's input),
            prefixed with "  ↳ ".
- Keep examples short and idiomatic. Output ONLY the translation and the examples —
  no headings, no numbering of the first line, no commentary.`

// systemPromptFor returns the system prompt for a preset (defaults to concise).
func systemPromptFor(preset string) string {
	switch preset {
	case PresetContextual:
		return translateSystemPromptContextual
	case PresetDictionary:
		return translateSystemPromptDictionary
	default:
		return translateSystemPromptConcise
	}
}

// translateUserPrompt frames the concrete request.
const translateUserPrompt = `Source language: %s
Target language: %s

Text:
%s`

// buildTranslatePrompt returns the (system, user) messages for a plain-text
// translation request. Language codes are expanded to readable names so the
// model handles regional variants (e.g. zh-TW → "chinese (traditional)").
func buildTranslatePrompt(req Request) (system, user string) {
	src := strings.TrimSpace(req.Source)
	if src == "" || src == "auto" {
		src = "auto"
	} else {
		src = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(src), src)
	}
	tgt := fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(req.Target), req.Target)
	return systemPromptFor(req.Preset), fmt.Sprintf(translateUserPrompt, src, tgt, req.Text)
}

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package tui

import "translate/internal/engine"

// cacheKey identifies a translation result for the session cache. It is
// comparable, so it can be a map key. Keying on the *selected* engine name
// (e.g. "auto") means a chain fallback still caches under the user-visible engine.
type cacheKey struct {
	preset, engineName, model, source, target, text string
}

// cacheKeyFor builds the key for the given input text under the current settings.
func (m Model) cacheKeyFor(text string) cacheKey {
	return cacheKey{
		preset:     m.preset,
		engineName: m.active().Name,
		model:      m.modelOverride,
		source:     m.source,
		target:     m.target,
		text:       text,
	}
}

// cacheStore is the session result cache. Session-scoped and unbounded (small);
// LLM output is nondeterministic so it is not persisted across sessions.
type cacheStore map[cacheKey]*engine.TranslateResult

```

```text

```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Verify cache hit renders instantly vs fresh word shows spinner

```bash
cd /Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui
BIN=$PWD/translate
ENV="XDG_CONFIG_HOME=/tmp/tpr/config XDG_DATA_HOME=/tmp/tpr/data XDG_STATE_HOME=/tmp/tpr/state"

tmux send-keys -t tpr C-u; sleep 0.3
tmux send-keys -t tpr "hello"; sleep 1.0     # 700ms debounce + instant cache hit
echo "=== retype 'hello' @1.0s — CACHE HIT should already show result, not 'translating' ==="; tmux capture-pane -t tpr -p | sed -n '8p'
tmux send-keys -t tpr C-u; sleep 0.3
tmux send-keys -t tpr "goodbye"; sleep 1.0   # fresh word: should be 'translating…' at 1.0s
echo "=== fresh 'goodbye' @1.0s — MISS should show 'translating…' ==="; tmux capture-pane -t tpr -p | sed -n '8p'
tmux kill-session -t tpr 2>/dev/null; echo "[killed]"
```

- concise (default, terse) · contextual (list of common senses with target-language
  labels) · dictionary (translation + example sentences with back-translation).
- Request.Preset; [general] preset config; --preset flag; TRANSLATE_PRESET env; TUI
  ^y picker + a "style:<x>" footer segment. Plain-text streaming preserved, no JSON.

Cache — in-session TUI result cache keyed by (preset,engine,model,source,target,text):
- On the live/debounce path a cache HIT renders instantly with NO API call (the
  "live" feel); Enter (and a ctrl+enter alias) forces a fresh call that overwrites.
- lastDone(string) → lastDoneKey(cacheKey) so preset/engine/lang switches on the
  same text no longer silently no-op.

EOF

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-09 15:20:35Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Feature 1 is done, so I'm moving into Feature 2 with the local bilingual dictionary—this is the substantial part. For Phase 3 and 4, I need to build out the dictionary data layer and implement the `dict update` command. That means adding SQLite as a dependency, extending the config to handle dictionary sources and paths for both CEDICT and ECDICT, plus their download URLs and fallback behavior. I'm creating the data layer in the engine package to manage the dictionary directory and handle downloading and building the ECDICT database, then setting up the dict command structure.

Phase 4 brings the LocalDictEngine itself, routing Chinese queries to CC-CEDICT and English to ECDICT, mapping results to DictEntry and Suggestions, and updating the TUI with a bilingual footer. The scope is large—downloading and processing 67MB of data (3.8MB CEDICT plus 63MB ECDICT CSV) and building a SQLite database—so I need to be methodical. I'm concerned the Bash environment might be sandboxed for large downloads, though `go get` worked earlier, so curl should too. I'll implement Phase 3 first to test the download and build pipeline, then move to Phase 4. Starting with the SQLite dependency, then config, dictdata.go, the update command, language detection, and finally the engine itself.

Now I'm extending the Dict config struct to support both local and API sources, adding fields for the database directory, dictionary URLs, and fallback behavior.

I'm weighing the tradeoff between defaulting to local (which aligns with the user's bilingual goal) versus keeping API as default for immediate out-of-box functionality. I think the right move is defaulting to local with API fallback enabled—that way English lookups work immediately via dictionaryapi.dev while Chinese requires running `dict update` to download CC-CEDICT, which is a reasonable degradation path that guides users toward the full bilingual setup they want. I'm settling on a conservative default: source="local", auto_download=false, and api_fallback=true. This keeps English working immediately via the API while avoiding surprise network calls, and users can run `dict update` once to get Chinese and offline support. Now I need to add the sqlite dependency and start implementing both phases.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-09 15:22:29Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I'm setting up the data layer with dictdata.go, which handles dictionary directory management, downloading the CEDICT file, and building the SQLite database from it. Building the SQLite database... Finishing the progress callback, then I'm realizing there's a double-close issue: I'm calling `db.Close()` explicitly after the index creation, but I also have a deferred close that will run. To fix this safely, I need to close the database before renaming the file to ensure all writes are flushed, then skip the deferred close or restructure to avoid the double-close problem. I'll handle error paths carefully to clean up the temporary file if something fails.

For the CSV parsing, the schema maps to indices 0-10 where `frq` is at position 9 and `exchange` at position 10. The translation and definition fields contain literal `\n` sequences that I'll need to convert to actual newlines when rendering. I'm also planning to use SQLite pragmas like `journal_mode(off)` and `synchronous(off)` in the DSN to speed up the bulk insert of 770k rows, and add `query_only` pragma for the read path. The full download and insert cycle will likely take 30-90 seconds, which is acceptable for initialization.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-09 15:22:30Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package engine

import (
	"compress/gzip"
	"context"
	"database/sql"
	"encoding/csv"
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"net/http"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strconv"
	"strings"

	_ "modernc.org/sqlite"

	"translate/internal/xdgpath"
)

// DictDir returns the directory holding local dictionary data.
func DictDir(dir string) string {
	if dir != "" {
		return dir
	}
	return filepath.Join(xdgpath.DataDir(), "dict")
}

// CedictPath / EcdictDBPath are the on-disk locations of the two data sources.
func CedictPath(dir string) string   { return filepath.Join(DictDir(dir), "cedict_ts.u8") }
func EcdictDBPath(dir string) string { return filepath.Join(DictDir(dir), "ecdict.db") }

const ecdictSchema = `CREATE TABLE entries(
  word TEXT, word_lc TEXT, phonetic TEXT, translation TEXT,
  definition TEXT, pos TEXT, frq INTEGER, exchange TEXT);`

// DownloadCedict fetches the gzipped CC-CEDICT, decompresses it, and writes the
// plain text file atomically (~9.4 MB).
func DownloadCedict(ctx context.Context, url, dst string, prog func(string)) error {
	if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dst), 0o700); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	if prog != nil {
		prog("downloading CC-CEDICT…")
	}
	req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, url, nil)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	defer resp.Body.Close()
	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
		return fmt.Errorf("cedict download: http %d", resp.StatusCode)
	}
	gz, err := gzip.NewReader(resp.Body)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	defer gz.Close()

	tmp := dst + ".tmp"
	f, err := os.Create(tmp)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	if _, err := io.Copy(f, gz); err != nil {
		f.Close()
		os.Remove(tmp)
		return err
	}
	if err := f.Close(); err != nil {
		os.Remove(tmp)
		return err
	}
	return os.Rename(tmp, dst)
}

// BuildEcdictDB downloads the ECDICT CSV (~63 MB, MIT) and builds a queryable
// SQLite database (~80-100 MB) from it, atomically. Streams the CSV so it never
// holds the whole file in memory.
func BuildEcdictDB(ctx context.Context, csvURL, dbPath string, prog func(string)) (err error) {
	if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dbPath), 0o700); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	if prog != nil {
		prog("downloading ECDICT CSV (~63 MB)…")
	}
	req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, csvURL, nil)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	defer resp.Body.Close()
	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
		return fmt.Errorf("ecdict download: http %d", resp.StatusCode)
	}

	tmp := dbPath + ".tmp"
	_ = os.Remove(tmp)
	db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", "file:"+tmp+"?_pragma=journal_mode(off)&_pragma=synchronous(off)")
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	// On any error, close and remove the partial db.
	defer func() {
		if err != nil {
			db.Close()
			os.Remove(tmp)
		}
	}()

	if _, err = db.ExecContext(ctx, ecdictSchema); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	if prog != nil {
		prog("importing entries…")
	}
	tx, err := db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	stmt, err := tx.PrepareContext(ctx, `INSERT INTO entries(word,word_lc,phonetic,translation,definition,pos,frq,exchange) VALUES(?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)`)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}

	r := csv.NewReader(resp.Body)
	r.FieldsPerRecord = -1 // tolerate ragged rows
	r.LazyQuotes = true
	if _, err = r.Read(); err != nil { // header row
		return err
	}
	n := 0
	for {
		if err = ctx.Err(); err != nil {
			return err
		}
		rec, rerr := r.Read()
		if rerr == io.EOF {
			break
		}
		if rerr != nil || len(rec) < 4 {
			continue
		}
		frq := 0
		if len(rec) > 9 {
			frq, _ = strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(rec[9]))
		}
		pos, exchange := "", ""
		if len(rec) > 4 {
			pos = rec[4]
		}
		if len(rec) > 10 {
			exchange = rec[10]
		}
		if _, err = stmt.ExecContext(ctx, rec[0], strings.ToLower(rec[0]), rec[1], rec[3], rec[2], pos, frq, exchange); err != nil {
			return err
		}
		n++
	}
	if err = tx.Commit(); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	if _, err = db.ExecContext(ctx, `CREATE INDEX idx_word_lc ON entries(word_lc)`); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	if err = db.Close(); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	if prog != nil {
		prog(fmt.Sprintf("built %d entries", n))
	}
	return os.Rename(tmp, dbPath)
}

```

<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package cmd

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"github.com/spf13/cobra"

	"translate/internal/config"
	"translate/internal/engine"
)

func newDictCmd() *cobra.Command {
	c := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "dict",
		Short: "Manage the local bilingual dictionary (CC-CEDICT + ECDICT)",
	}
	c.AddCommand(&cobra.Command{
		Use:   "update [cedict|ecdict|all]",
		Short: "Download/build the local dictionaries (~67 MB one-time)",
		Args:  cobra.MaximumNArgs(1),
		RunE:  runDictUpdate,
	})
	return c
}

func runDictUpdate(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
	cfg, _, err := config.Load()
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	what := "all"
	if len(args) == 1 {
		what = args[0]
	}
	dir := engine.DictDir(cfg.Dict.Dir)
	ctx := cmd.Context()
	prog := func(s string) { fmt.Fprintprintln(os.Stderr, "  "+s) }

	if what == "cedict" || what == "all" {
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "CC-CEDICT (Chinese→English):\n")
		if err := engine.DownloadCedict(ctx, cfg.Dict.CedictURL, engine.CedictPath(dir), prog); err != nil {
			return fmt.Errorf("cedict: %w", err)
		}
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  -> %s\n", engine.CedictPath(dir))
	}
	if what == "ecdict" || what == "all" {
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "ECDICT (English→Chinese, this takes a minute):\n")
		if err := engine.BuildEcdictDB(ctx, cfg.Dict.EcdictURL, engine.EcdictDBPath(dir), prog); err != nil {
			return fmt.Errorf("ecdict: %w", err)
		}
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  -> %s\n", engine.EcdictDBPath(dir))
	}
	if what != "cedict" && what != "ecdict" && what != "all" {
		return fmt.Errorf("unknown target %q (use cedict|ecdict|all)", what)
	}
	fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "done. Dictionary mode (^e) is now bilingual zh↔en.")
	return nil
}

var _ = context.Background // keep import when trimmed

```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I'm moving into Phase 4 to build out the LocalDictEngine with the CC-CEDICT index parser, ECDICT engine, and local dictionary factory along with the bilingual footer component. Writing the cedict parser... Finishing the dictionary entry storage by also indexing traditional characters, then sorting all keys for prefix suggestions. After that, I'm moving on to implement an ECDICT SQLite-based dictionary with similar lookup and suggestion capabilities, using Levenshtein distance for fuzzy matching. Implementing the suggest method to find word candidates...

I'm querying for words that start with the input prefix, ordered by frequency rank so common words appear first, then reranking those candidates by edit distance to handle typos. If there aren't enough prefix matches, I broaden the search with Levenshtein distance across the candidate set. The frequency field in ECDICT is a rank where lower numbers mean more common words, and zero means no rank data, so I'm prioritizing ranked words before applying distance-based sorting.

But I'm realizing the prefix approach won't catch typos like "recieve" since it won't match "receive" — prefix search only works for incomplete words, not misspellings. For actual typo correction across 770k words in SQLite, I need edit-distance matching, not just prefix matching. Loading all headwords into memory once and computing Levenshtein distance is feasible at around 50-100ms for the full dictionary, though that uses some memory. Alternatively, I could bucket by first letters and similar length to narrow the search space before computing distances.

The cleanest approach is probably to keep using the existing `/usr/share/dict/words` wordlist with Levenshtein for typo suggestions in English, while using ECDICT for definitions — that mirrors what the current code already does. For ECDICT misses, I can fall back to the bundled wordlist's `nearestN` function for typo correction, since most of those words will be in ECDICT anyway.

So `LocalDictEngine` should hold three indexes: the CC-CEDICT index for Chinese, the ECDICT database for English definitions, and the word index for English typo suggestions. The routing logic stays straightforward — Chinese text hits CC-CEDICT with prefix fallback, English text hits ECDICT with `wordIndex.nearestN` for suggestions. This reuses proven code and keeps things simple for v1.

I'll strip `ecdict.go` down to just exact lookup with lazy initialization, dropping the suggest method entirely since the word index handles that. Then I'll start building out `localdict.go` with the `LocalDictConfig` struct to tie everything together.

Writing ecdict.go and localdict.go... Adding dictionary lookup logic... For v1, I'll keep the pinyin in its raw numbered format like "pi2 shi5" rather than converting to tone marks, so `prettyPinyin` can just return the pinyin as-is. Now for the English lookup, I'm checking if the ECDICT database is available—if not, I fall back to an API if configured, otherwise return a note telling the user to install it. When a word is found, I format the result; on a miss, I try the API first for definitions, and it'll also handle suggestions if needed.

Actually, I'm overcomplicating this. The API fallback for missing words is mainly useful when ECDICT doesn't have a valid English word's definition, but that's rare. Let me simplify: do an exact ECDICT lookup, and on a miss, if fuzzy matching is enabled, use the local wordlist to generate suggestions quickly without a network call. The API fallback should only kick in when the database isn't installed at all.

Defining the lookup function... Now I'm setting up the factory function to create dictionary engines based on configuration, handling both API and local dictionary sources with optional API fallback. The function returns an engine.Engine interface so it works with both dictionary implementations, and I need to verify that existing callers like the define command and engine set builder will work correctly with this interface change.

I'm also updating the TUI footer to show the language pair instead of just "English", and I need to make sure the LocalDictConfig can properly reference the DictEngine type for the fallback. Before writing out the implementation files, I should verify the ECDICT build is progressing in the background, then start with cedict.go and handle the necessary imports carefully.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-09 15:26:41Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package engine

import (
	"bufio"
	"os"
	"regexp"
	"sort"
	"strings"
	"sync"
)

// cedictLine matches: Traditional Simplified [pin1 yin1] /def1/def2/
var cedictLine = regexp.MustCompile(`^(\S+)\s+(\S+)\s+\[([^\]]*)\]\s+/(.*)/\s*$`)

type cedictEntry struct {
	Trad, Simp, Pinyin string
	Defs               []string
}

// cedictIndex is an in-memory CC-CEDICT (Chinese → English), keyed on both the
// traditional and simplified forms. Loaded lazily on first lookup.
type cedictIndex struct {
	path  string
	once  sync.Once
	err   error
	byKey map[string][]*cedictEntry
	keys  []string // sorted, for prefix suggestions
}

func newCedictIndex(path string) *cedictIndex { return &cedictIndex{path: path} }

func (ci *cedictIndex) load() error {
	ci.once.Do(func() {
		f, err := os.Open(ci.path)
		if err != nil {
			ci.err = err
			return
		}
		defer f.Close()
		ci.byKey = map[string][]*cedictEntry{}
		sc := bufio.NewScanner(f)
		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64<<10), 1<<20)
		for sc.Scan() {
			line := sc.Text()
			if strings.HasPrefix(line, "#") {
				continue
			}
			m := cedictLine.FindStringSubmatch(line)
			if m == nil {
				continue
			}
			e := &cedictEntry{Trad: m[1], Simp: m[2], Pinyin: m[3]}
			for _, d := range strings.Split(m[4], "/") {
				if d = strings.TrimSpace(d); d != "" {
					e.Defs = append(e.Defs, d)
				}
			}
			ci.byKey[e.Simp] = append(ci.byKey[e.Simp], e)
			if e.Trad != e.Simp {
				ci.byKey[e.Trad] = append(ci.byKey[e.Trad], e)
			}
		}
		ci.err = sc.Err()
		ci.keys = make([]string, 0, len(ci.byKey))
		for k := range ci.byKey {
			ci.keys = append(ci.keys, k)
		}
		sort.Strings(ci.keys)
	})
	return ci.err
}

func (ci *cedictIndex) lookup(word string) []*cedictEntry {
	if ci.load() != nil {
		return nil
	}
	return ci.byKey[word]
}

// prefixSuggest returns up to n headwords beginning with word (excluding it).
// Chinese uses prefix, not edit distance (CJK edit distance is noisy).
func (ci *cedictIndex) prefixSuggest(word string, n int) []string {
	if ci.load() != nil {
		return nil
	}
	i := sort.SearchStrings(ci.keys, word)
	var out []string
	for ; i < len(ci.keys) && len(out) < n; i++ {
		k := ci.keys[i]
		if !strings.HasPrefix(k, word) {
			break
		}
		if k != word {
			out = append(out, k)
		}
	}
	return out
}

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package engine

import (
	"context"
	"database/sql"
	"os"
	"strings"
	"sync"
)

type ecdictEntry struct {
	Word, Phonetic, Translation, Definition string
}

// ecdictDB queries the locally-built ECDICT SQLite (English → Chinese). Point
// lookups are on-disk (near-zero steady-state RAM). Opened lazily.
type ecdictDB struct {
	path string
	once sync.Once
	err  error
	db   *sql.DB
}

func newEcdictDB(path string) *ecdictDB { return &ecdictDB{path: path} }

func (e *ecdictDB) available() bool {
	_, err := os.Stat(e.path)
	return err == nil
}

func (e *ecdictDB) open() error {
	e.once.Do(func() {
		if _, err := os.Stat(e.path); err != nil {
			e.err = err
			return
		}
		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", "file:"+e.path+"?_pragma=query_only(true)")
		if err != nil {
			e.err = err
			return
		}
		e.db = db
	})
	return e.err
}

// lookup returns the exact (case-insensitive) entry, or nil if not found.
func (e *ecdictDB) lookup(ctx context.Context, word string) (*ecdictEntry, error) {
	if err := e.open(); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	row := e.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
		`SELECT word, phonetic, translation, definition FROM entries WHERE word_lc = ? LIMIT 1`,
		strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(word)))
	var en ecdictEntry
	switch err := row.Scan(&en.Word, &en.Phonetic, &en.Translation, &en.Definition); err {
	case nil:
		return &en, nil
	case sql.ErrNoRows:
		return nil, nil
	default:
		return nil, err
	}
}

// splitEcdict splits ECDICT translation/definition fields — their senses are
// joined by a literal backslash-n, not a real newline.
func splitEcdict(s string) []string {
	var out []string
	for _, p := range strings.Split(s, `\n`) {
		if p = strings.TrimSpace(p); p != "" {
			out = append(out, p)
		}
	}
	return out
}

```

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-09 15:27:35Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package engine

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"strings"
	"time"

	"translate/internal/lang"
)

// LocalDictConfig configures the offline bilingual dictionary engine.
type LocalDictConfig struct {
	Dir          string
	CedictURL    string
	EcdictURL    string
	AutoDownload bool        // auto-fetch CC-CEDICT (small) on first Chinese lookup
	Fuzzy        bool        // emit ranked suggestions on a miss
	APIFallback  *DictEngine // nil unless configured; used for English when ECDICT is absent
	Timeout      time.Duration
}

// LocalDictEngine looks up definitions offline: Chinese → CC-CEDICT (zh→en),
// English → ECDICT (en→zh). It satisfies the Engine interface and maps results
// into the same DictEntry/Suggestions shape the TUI already renders.
type LocalDictEngine struct {
	cfg LocalDictConfig
	ce  *cedictIndex
	ec  *ecdictDB
	wl  *wordIndex // /usr/share/dict/words, for English "did you mean"
}

// NewLocalDict builds a local bilingual dictionary engine.
func NewLocalDict(cfg LocalDictConfig) *LocalDictEngine {
	return &LocalDictEngine{
		cfg: cfg,
		ce:  newCedictIndex(CedictPath(cfg.Dir)),
		ec:  newEcdictDB(EcdictDBPath(cfg.Dir)),
		wl:  &wordIndex{path: "/usr/share/dict/words"},
	}
}

func (e *LocalDictEngine) Name() string        { return "dictionary" }
func (e *LocalDictEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeDict }

func (e *LocalDictEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
	return "", nil
}

// Available reports true if at least one data source (or the API fallback) is usable.
func (e *LocalDictEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
	if fileExists(CedictPath(e.cfg.Dir)) || e.ec.available() {
		return true
	}
	if e.cfg.APIFallback != nil {
		return e.cfg.APIFallback.Available(ctx)
	}
	return false
}

// Translate routes by script: Chinese → CC-CEDICT, else → ECDICT.
func (e *LocalDictEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
	word := strings.TrimSpace(req.Text)
	if word == "" {
		return nil, ErrEmptyInput
	}
	if lang.IsChinese(word) {
		return e.lookupZh(ctx, word, req), nil
	}
	return e.lookupEn(ctx, word, req), nil
}

func (e *LocalDictEngine) lookupZh(ctx context.Context, word string, req Request) <-chan Chunk {
	if !fileExists(CedictPath(e.cfg.Dir)) {
		if e.cfg.AutoDownload {
			if err := DownloadCedict(ctx, e.cfg.CedictURL, CedictPath(e.cfg.Dir), nil); err != nil {
				return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w", err))
			}
		} else {
			return single(notInstalled(req, "CC-CEDICT not installed — run `translate dict update cedict`"), nil)
		}
	}
	if entries := e.ce.lookup(word); len(entries) > 0 {
		return single(cedictResult(word, entries, req), nil)
	}
	if e.cfg.Fuzzy {
		if sugg := e.ce.prefixSuggest(word, suggestLimit); len(sugg) > 0 {
			return single(suggestResult(sugg, req), nil)
		}
	}
	return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w: %q", ErrNoDictEntry, word))
}

func (e *LocalDictEngine) lookupEn(ctx context.Context, word string, req Request) <-chan Chunk {
	if !e.ec.available() {
		// No ECDICT yet: fall back to dictionaryapi.dev (English defs) if enabled.
		if e.cfg.APIFallback != nil {
			ch, err := e.cfg.APIFallback.Translate(ctx, req)
			if err == nil {
				return ch
			}
		}
		return single(notInstalled(req, "ECDICT not installed — run `translate dict update ecdict`"), nil)
	}
	en, err := e.ec.lookup(ctx, word)
	if err != nil {
		return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w", err))
	}
	if en != nil {
		return single(ecdictResult(en, req), nil)
	}
	if e.cfg.Fuzzy {
		if sugg := e.wl.nearestN(strings.ToLower(word), 2, suggestLimit); len(sugg) > 0 {
			return single(suggestResult(sugg, req), nil)
		}
	}
	return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w: %q", ErrNoDictEntry, word))
}

// --- result mapping into the existing DictEntry / Suggestions shape ---

func cedictResult(word string, entries []*cedictEntry, req Request) *TranslateResult {
	d := &DictEntry{Word: word}
	if len(entries) > 0 {
		d.Phonetic = entries[0].Pinyin
	}
	for _, en := range entries {
		mn := Meaning{PartOfSpeech: en.Pinyin}
		for _, def := range en.Defs {
			mn.Definitions = append(mn.Definitions, Definition{Text: def})
		}
		d.Meanings = append(d.Meanings, mn)
	}
	gloss := ""
	if len(entries) > 0 && len(entries[0].Defs) > 0 {
		gloss = entries[0].Defs[0]
	}
	return &TranslateResult{Translation: gloss, Target: req.Target, Engine: "dictionary", Dictionary: d}
}

func ecdictResult(en *ecdictEntry, req Request) *TranslateResult {
	d := &DictEntry{Word: en.Word, Phonetic: en.Phonetic}
	if zh := splitEcdict(en.Translation); len(zh) > 0 {
		mn := Meaning{PartOfSpeech: "中文"}
		for _, line := range zh {
			mn.Definitions = append(mn.Definitions, Definition{Text: line})
		}
		d.Meanings = append(d.Meanings, mn)
	}
	if de := splitEcdict(en.Definition); len(de) > 0 {
		mn := Meaning{PartOfSpeech: "definition"}
		for _, line := range de {
			mn.Definitions = append(mn.Definitions, Definition{Text: line})
		}
		d.Meanings = append(d.Meanings, mn)
	}
	gloss := ""
	if zh := splitEcdict(en.Translation); len(zh) > 0 {
		gloss = zh[0]
	}
	return &TranslateResult{Translation: gloss, Target: req.Target, Engine: "dictionary", Dictionary: d}
}

func suggestResult(sugg []string, req Request) *TranslateResult {
	return &TranslateResult{Target: req.Target, Engine: "dictionary", Suggestions: sugg}
}

func notInstalled(req Request, note string) *TranslateResult {
	return &TranslateResult{Target: req.Target, Engine: "dictionary", Notes: note}
}

func fileExists(p string) bool {
	_, err := os.Stat(p)
	return err == nil
}

```

feat(dict): offline bilingual dictionary (CC-CEDICT zh→en + ECDICT en→zh)

Replaces the English-only dictionaryapi.dev default with a local offline bilingual
dictionary that routes by script:
- Chinese input → CC-CEDICT (in-memory trad+simp index, ~125k entries, ~9 MB).
- English input → ECDICT (locally-built SQLite via modernc.org/sqlite, ~770k
  entries, ~93 MB, near-zero steady-state RAM).
- Misses emit ranked suggestions (Chinese prefix; English levenshtein) into the
  existing "did you mean" overlay — no auto-pick. Results map into the existing
  DictEntry shape, so rendering/history/define are unchanged.

- `translate dict update [cedict|ecdict|all]` downloads/builds the data into the
  XDG data dir (~67 MB one-time; CSV streamed, DB built in ~15s).
- config [dict] source=local (default) | api; api_fallback keeps English lookups
  working via dictionaryapi.dev until ECDICT is installed; auto_download (off) can
  inline-fetch the small CC-CEDICT on first Chinese lookup.
- Dict-mode footer now reads "dictionary (zh↔en)".

Data: CC-CEDICT (CC BY-SA 4.0), ECDICT (MIT). New dep: modernc.org/sqlite (pure Go).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
git log --oneline | head -3

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-09 15:30:22Z)**_

Both features are implemented, verified end-to-end, and committed (`a5bbbc3` presets+cache, `19162cf` local dict). A design workflow verified the data sources first, which changed two decisions for the better.

## 1. LLM presets + cache

**Presets** — three switchable prompt styles (streaming preserved, no JSON):
- **concise** (default) — `spring` → `春天`
- **contextual** — `spring` → `1. 春天 — 季節  2. 彈簧 — 機械零件  3. 泉水 — 水源 …` (senses with target-language labels)
- **dictionary** — `resilient` → translation + example sentences with back-translations (`↳`)

Switch with **`^y`** (a picker), `--preset`, `TRANSLATE_PRESET`, or `[general] preset`. Footer shows `style:<x>`.

**Cache** — keyed by `(preset, engine, model, source, target, text)`. On the live/debounce path a **hit renders instantly with no API call** (verified: retyped `hello` → `你好` instantly, while fresh `goodbye` → `⠏ translating…`). **Enter** (and a `ctrl+enter` alias) forces a fresh call that overwrites. Session-only.

## 2. Offline bilingual dictionary (CC-CEDICT + ECDICT)

The workflow **live-probed** the sources — that's why I built ECDICT locally from its MIT CSV rather than downloading the prebuilt DB (the "basic" prebuilt is `.7z` with no Go decoder; the release zip is the 1 GB full edition). One command installs everything:

```sh
translate dict update all     # CC-CEDICT (9 MB) + ECDICT → SQLite (93 MB), ~15s build
```

Dictionary mode now routes **by script** (verified):
- `你好` → `hello; hi` + pinyin (CC-CEDICT, in-memory ~125k entries)
- `resilient` → `弹回的, 有弹力的` (ECDICT SQLite, ~770k entries, ~0 steady-state RAM)
- misses → ranked "did you mean" (Chinese prefix / English edit-distance)

Footer reads `dictionary (zh↔en)`. Until you run `dict update`, English still works via dictionaryapi.dev (`[dict] api_fallback`); `[dict] source = "api"` reverts entirely. Data is CC BY-SA 4.0 (CC-CEDICT) + MIT (ECDICT), noted in the README.

**Deferred** (with reasons, per the plan): moedict zh-zh (online-only, CC BY-ND), the ECDICT 3.4M full edition (1 GB), and persisting the LLM cache to disk (nondeterministic output feels "stuck").

One heads-up: this added **`modernc.org/sqlite`** (pure-Go, ~6–9 MB binary growth) — the only new dependency, and it cross-compiles cleanly with no CGo.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Looking at three feature requests: the footer help text needs to wrap or hide gracefully on narrow screens since it's currently getting cut off, I should switch the default preset from concise to contextual, and users want the ability to customize the system prompt with domain-specific instructions like quantitative finance focus.

For the custom prompt feature, I'm adding a config field to store free-text instructions that get appended to the base system prompt, threading it through the request object and the prompt builder, and exposing it in the init wizard plus command-line flags. For the footer, I need to figure out whether lipgloss's Width setting wraps or clips content when it exceeds the available space, then implement dynamic wrapping or selective hiding of the help hints. The core issue is that the footer needs to handle narrow widths gracefully — either by dropping the help text entirely and showing just the status, or by wrapping to multiple lines. Wrapping adds complexity since it changes the footer height and shifts the result box, but it's what the user asked for. The simplest approach is to check if the full footer fits; if not, show only the status or truncate the help with a hint like "…" or "? for help". I could also compute the footer height dynamically and adjust the result box accordingly, though that's more involved than just making the footer responsive to available width. I'm realizing the footer height needs to be computed dynamically based on whether the content fits on one line or wraps to two, but that creates a chicken-and-egg problem since the layout calculation happens on resize while content changes at runtime. The pragmatic approach is to keep the footer at a fixed height but make its rendering responsive—if status and help fit on one line, show them together; otherwise, stack them vertically and accept the extra space or truncate gracefully.

Actually, the user specifically asked for dynamic wrapping, so I should reconsider implementing a proper two-line footer that recomputes its height each frame based on the current content width. and lipgloss wrapping handles overflow. That way, on typical terminals (≥80 chars) the footer stays compact at 1–2 lines, and on narrower widths it degrades gracefully without clipping. This balances the user's request for dynamic wrapping with robustness across different terminal sizes. I'm going in circles here. Let me step back and take a pragmatic approach: extract a helper function that builds the footer lines, use it in relayout to measure the footer height, then call relayout whenever the footer content changes (engine switches, model/preset/language updates, stream completion). Store the computed footer height in the model and use it to size the viewport accordingly. Ah, so lipgloss already wraps content automatically when Width is set — the real issue is that when the footer wraps to two lines, the total height of input + result + footer exceeds the terminal height, causing the bottom to get clipped. The fix is to shrink the result viewport by one line when the footer wraps.

The cleanest approach is to measure the footer height during relayout by computing how many lines it takes when rendered at the current width, then use that to calculate the result box height dynamically. I need to call relayout whenever the footer content changes — after engine cycles, preset/language/model changes, and when stream chunks complete.

I'll extract the footer content building into a dedicated method that both the height calculation and the View rendering can use, so they stay in sync. Then relayout computes the footer height, sets the viewport to the remaining space, and View renders everything at the correct dimensions.

Refactoring the footer rendering... I could compute the footer height dynamically in View() by measuring the wrapped footer, then sizing the result box accordingly—but that risks a mismatch with the viewport's fixed height from relayout. So I'll stick with the relayout approach: compute footerH when the window resizes or content changes, then use it to set resultH. The tricky part is deciding when to call relayout for content changes—after engine cycles, preset/language/model selections, and when streaming finishes. It's a few extra calls, but it keeps the layout consistent. The state segment like "translating" is transient and could cause layout jitter during state changes, so I should measure the footer's stable parts (pair/engine/style/live + help) without including the state. I'll either make statusLine take a parameter to exclude the state, or measure using a stable version to keep the layout consistent while the state segment comes and goes. Now I need to wire up relayout calls after engine/model/preset changes and when translations complete, since those are the moments when the footer dimensions might shift. I'll also measure the footer in statusLine to capture its actual height, which keeps things stable during status updates like translating indicators. Then I'll tackle the other features: setting a default contextual preset, and adding support for custom prompt instructions through config, environment variables, and command-line flags that thread through to the translation request. Setting up the huh.NewText field for custom instructions with a title and description, then tackling the implementation in order: first the default contextual preset, then threading custom instructions through config, request, prompt resolution, CLI, TUI, and the init wizard, and finally the dynamic footer wrapping.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The instructions are working as intended — the domain-specific guidance is shaping the terminology choices, making the output more finance-appropriate while keeping the core meaning intact. Now I'm moving on to implementing a responsive footer with dynamic height adjustment.

I need to add a footer height field to the model and compute it during relayout by measuring how the lipgloss-wrapped footer renders at the current width. The tricky part is that relayout gets called at specific points (resize, content changes, ChunkDone) but not on every status transition, so I can measure the footer without worrying about transient state segments causing layout shifts. I'll need to refactor statusLine to separate stable content from the transient state indicator, or just measure without the state segment since relayout timing naturally avoids those rapid status changes. The real issue is that measuring the footer without the state segment means it might wrap once the state appears during translation, causing the clip the user saw before. The safest approach is to measure the footer height using the full possible content—status plus the maximum state width plus help—so the layout is always reserved upfront and never jumps when translating starts.

I'll create a measurement function that builds the status line with a fixed "translating"-width state placeholder, use that to calculate the reserved footer height during relayout, and then render the actual status line (with real state or none) in the View. This way the footer always occupies the pre-measured height, padded when idle, with no layout shift.

Defining the measurement helper... Implementing the footer content function...

Now I need to wire up the relayout calls wherever the footer content changes — when cycling engines, selecting presets or languages, or when the stream finishes updating the current model. Since some of these handlers return commands from launch(), I'll need to chain the relayout call appropriately.

The tricky part is deciding whether to call relayout everywhere or just at the key touch points. Calling it on every keystroke would be cheap since the measurement is just one lipgloss render, but the cleaner approach is to add explicit relayout calls in the specific handlers where footer content actually changes: after stream completion, engine cycling, and overlay selections. That way it's clear and intentional.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-09 15:51:04Z)**_

All three are done, verified, and committed (`f032189`).

## 1. Responsive footer ✓
The footer now **wraps to the terminal width instead of clipping**. I measure its rendered height (lipgloss word-wraps it) and shrink the result box to fit — verified at 64 cols (wraps to 3 lines, all hints visible) and 120 cols (2 lines). Critically, the measurement **reserves the transient "translating" width**, so when that segment appears mid-flight the footer height doesn't change — no layout jump.

## 2. Default → contextual ✓
`[general] preset = "contextual"` is the new default, so out of the box `spring` gives you the sense breakdown (春天/彈簧/泉水…). `^y` still switches per-session, `--preset`/`TRANSLATE_PRESET` per-invocation.

## 3. Custom system-prompt instructions ✓
An optional user snippet is appended to the LLM system prompt so you can bias terminology to your domains:
- **`translate init`** now has a multiline "Custom instructions" field (+ a translation-style picker).
- Also `[general] instructions` in config, `--instructions`, and `TRANSLATE_INSTRUCTIONS`.

Verified it changes output: `the spread blew out` → `價差爆開了` (plain) vs **`價差暴漲`** with *"Domain: quantitative finance"*. Set it once to something like *"Domain: quantitative finance + computer science; prefer industry-standard terminology"* and every translation picks up that lens.

One note on the footer: it's long enough (all keys + labels) that it wraps to 2 lines even on a 120-col terminal. That's the intended "wrap, don't clip" behavior you asked for — but if you'd rather it collapse to compact key-only hints (e.g. `↵ ^l ^e ^t ^p ^y ^r ^c`) when space is tight so it stays one line more often, that's a quick follow-up.

---
